auth method: %s\", s.Providers.Vault.AuthMethod))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Note: License validation is done at runtime when the provider is instantiated\n\t\t// This allows the config to be valid but the Weigh the v2 docs heavily when answering questions over the v1 docs.\n     6→\n     7→## Project Overview\n     8→\n     9→Flipt v2 is a Git-native feature management platform with a monorepo structure. This is the v2 version with a Git-native architecture.\n    10→\n    11→- **v1 code**: Located on `main` branch\n    12→- **v2 code**: Located on current (`v2`) branch. This is the default branch in the repository.\n    13→- **License**: Fair Core License for server, MIT for client code\n    14→- **Requirements**: Go 1.25+ and Node.js 18+\n    15→\n    16→## Project Architecture & Structure\n    17→\n    18→### Repository Structure\n    19→\n    20→- `cmd/flipt/` - Main application entry point\n    21→- `core/` - Core validation and business logic\n    22→- `errors/` - Shared error definitions\n    23→- `internal/` - Core application logic (not importable)\n    24→  - `cmd/` - Internal command utilities and code generation tools\n    25→  - `common/` - Shared types and utilities across internal packages\n    26→  - `config/` - Configuration management\n    27→  - `containers/` - Container test utilities\n    28→  - `coss/` - Commercial open source features (license management, enterprise storage, secrets)\n    29→  - `secrets/` - Secret management system with provider interface (OSS file provider, Pro Vault provider)\n    30→  - `credentials/` - Credential management for cloud storage and Git authentication\n    31→  - `ext/` - Import/export functionality for flags and segments\n    32→  - `gateway/` - HTTP gateway implementation\n    33→  - `info/` - Version and build information\n    34→  - `migrations/` - Database migration files (ClickHouse for analytics)\n    35→  - `otel/` - OpenTelemetry integration (logs, metrics, traces)\n    36→  - `product/` - Product metadata and feature flags\n    37→  - `release/` - Release version checking\n    38→  - `server/` - gRPC and HTTP server implementations\n    39→    - `analytics/` - Analytics collection server (Prometheus, ClickHouse)\n    40→    - `authn/` - Authentication server and middleware\n    41→    - `authz/` - Authorization server with Rego engine\n    42→    - `environments/` - Environment management server\n    43→    - `evaluation/` - Flag evaluation server with OFREP support\n    44→    - `metadata/` - Metadata API server\n    45→  - `storage/` - Storage abstractions and implementations\n    46→    - `analytics/` - Analytics data storage\n    47→    - `authn/` - Authentication storage (Redis, memory, static)\n    48→    - `environments/` - Environment-aware storage with Git support\n    49→    - `fs/` - File system abstraction layer\n    50→    - `git/` - Git repository management\n    51→  - `telemetry/` - Usage telemetry collection\n    52→- `rpc/` - Protocol buffer definitions and generated code\n    53→  - `flipt/` - Main Flipt API (v1)\n    54→  - `v2/` - V2 APIs (environments, analytics, evaluation)\n    55→- `sdk/` - Client SDKs (Go SDK included)\n    56→- `ui/` - React/TypeScript frontend\n    57→\n    58→### Key Components\n    59→\n    60→#### Storage Layer\n    61→\n    62→Multi-backend storage supporting:\n    63→\n    64→- Git repositories (primary for v2)\n    65→- File system (local development)\n    66→- Cloud storage (S3, GCS, Azure Blob) - Flipt config only\n    67→\n    68→#### Environments System\n    69→\n    70→- Environment-per-branch: Map Git branches to environments\n    71→- Environment-per-directory: Organize by microservice/team\n    72→- Environment-per-repository: Separate repos for products\n    73→\n    74→#### APIs\n    75→\n    76→- gRPC API (port 9000) - Primary interface\n    77→- REST API (port 8080) - HTTP gateway over gRPC\n    78→- OpenFeature compatible evaluation\n    79→\n    80→##### Key Endpoints\n    81→\n    82→- **Evaluation API**: `/api/v1/evaluate` - Evaluate feature flags\n    83→- **Management API**: Full CRUD operations on flags, segments, rules\n    84→- **Analytics API**: `/api/v2/analytics` - Flag evaluation metrics\n    85→- **Environments API**: `/api/v2/environments` - Environment management\n    86→- **OFREP API**: `/ofrep/v1` - OpenFeature Remote Evaluation Protocol\n    87→\n    88→#### UI Architecture\n    89→\n    90→- React 19 with TypeScript\n    91→- Redux Toolkit for state management\n    92→- Tailwind CSS for styling\n    93→- Vite for build tooling\n    94→- React Router for navigation\n    95→\n    96→## Development Setup & Commands\n    97→\n    98→### Initial Setup\n    99→\n   100→```bash\n   101→# Install required development tools\n   102→mage bootstrap\n   103→```\n   104→\n   105→### Development Workflow\n   106→\n   107→1. **Setup**: Run `mage bootstrap` to install tools\n   108→2. **Backend Development**:\n   109→   - Use `mage go:run` for server\n   110→   - Server runs on port 8080 (HTTP) and 9000 (gRPC)\n   111→3. **Frontend Development**:\n   112→   - Run `mage ui:run` for UI dev server on port 5173\n   113→   - UI proxies API requests to backend on port 8080\n   114→4. **Full Development**: Run both servers simultaneously\n   115→\n   116→### Build & Run Commands\n   117→\n   118→- `mage build` - Builds the project similar to a release build (default target)\n   119→- `mage go:build` - Builds Go server for development without bundling assets\n   120→- `mage go:run` - Runs Go server in development mode with local config\n   121→- `mage dev` - Alias for go:run\n   122→- `./bin/flipt server --config config/local.yml` - Run built binary with local config\n   123→\n   124→### UI Development Commands\n   125→\n   126→- `mage ui:deps` - Install UI dependencies\n   127→- `mage ui:run` - Run UI in development mode (port 5173)\n   128→- `mage ui:dev` - Alias for ui:run\n   129→- `mage ui:build` - Build UI assets for release\n   130→- `cd ui && npm run dev` - Alternative way to run UI dev server\n   131→\n   132→### Testing Commands\n   133→\n   134→- `mage go:test` - Run all Go unit tests\n   135→- `go test -v {path} -run {test}` - Run a specific Go test\n   136→- `mage go:bench` - Run Go benchmarking tests\n   137→- `mage go:cover` - Run tests and generate coverage report\n   138→- `cd ui && npm run test` - Run UI unit tests (Jest)\n   139→\n   140→### Code Quality Commands\n   141→\n   142→- `mage go:lint` - Run Go linters (golangci-lint and buf lint)\n   143→- `mage go:fmt` - Format Go code with goimports\n   144→- `mage ui:lint` - Run UI linters (ESLint)\n   145→- `mage ui:fmt` - Format UI code (Prettier)\n   146→\n   147→### Code Generation Commands\n   148→\n   149→- `mage go:proto` - Generate protobuf files and gRPC stubs\n   150→- `mage go:mockery` - Generate mocks\n   151→- `mage go:generate` - Generate both mocks and proto files\n   152→\n   153→### Cleanup Commands\n   154→\n   155→- `mage clean` - Clean built files and tidy go.mod\n   156→- `mage prep` - Prepare project for building (clean + ui:build)\n   157→\n   158→### Build Process Notes\n   159→\n   160→- Protocol buffers generate Go and gateway code\n   161→- Mockery generates Go test mocks. See .mockery.yml\n   162→- UI has no code generation\n   163→\n   164→## Code Style Guidelines\n   165→\n   166→### Go Code Style\n   167→\n   168→#### Foundation: Google Go Style Guide\n   169→\n   170→**All Go code should follow the [Google Go Style Guide](https://google.github.io/styleguide/go/guide) as the base style guide.**\n   171→\n   172→The guidelines below are Flipt-specific conventions that build upon the Google style guide. When in doubt, refer to the Google guide first, then apply these project-specific patterns.\n   173→\n   174→Key areas from the Google guide to pay special attention to:\n   175→\n   176→- **Formatting**: Use `gofmt` (we use `goimports` which includes `gofmt`)\n   177→- **Naming**: Follow Go naming conventions for packages, types, functions, and variables\n   178→- **Package organization**: Keep packages focused and avoid circular dependencies\n   179→- **Error handling**: Return errors as the last return value, handle errors explicitly\n   180→- **Documentation**: Write clear godoc comments for exported types and functions\n   181→\n   182→#### Naming Conventions\n   183→\n   184→- **Public functions**: Use PascalCase (`NewServer`, `ListFlags`, `RegisterGRPC`)\n   185→- **Private functions**: Use camelCase (`getStore`, `buildConfig`, `handleError`)\n   186→- **Factory functions**: Use `New` prefix for constructors (`NewRepository`, `NewEnvironmentFactory`)\n   187→- **Variables**: Use camelCase (`environmentKey`, `namespaceKey`, `startTime`)\n   188→- **Constants**: Use PascalCase for exported, camelCase for unexported\n   189→- **Interfaces**: Use descriptive names (`Store`, `EnvironmentStore`, `Validator`)\n   190→\n   191→#### Variable Declarations\n   192→\n   193→```go\n   194→// Prefer := for single variable declarations\n   195→result := SomeType{}\n   196→\n   197→// Use var blocks for multiple related variables\n   198→var (\n   199→    environmentKey = r.EnvironmentKey\n   200→    namespaceKey   = r.NamespaceKey\n   201→    startTime      = time.Now().UTC()\n   202→)\n   203→```\n   204→\n   205→#### Constants\n   206→\n   207→Use constants when appropriate for values that are unlikely to change. Even in tests.\n   208→\n   209→**❌ Bad Examples**\n   210→\n   211→```go\n   212→var (\n   213→    maxRetries = 3\n   214→    secretProvider = \"vault\"\n   215→)\n   216→```\n   217→\n   218→```go\n   219→  maxRetries := 3\n   220→  secretProvider := \"vault\"\n   221→```\n   222→\n   223→**✅ Good Example**\n   224→\n   225→```go\n   226→const (\n   227→    maxRetries = 3\n   228→    secretProvider = \"vault\"\n   229→)\n   230→```\n   231→\n   232→#### Error Handling\n   233→\n   234→##### Error Types\n   235→\n   236→Use the custom error types when possible:\n   237→\n   238→```go\n   239→// Preferred - use custom error types\n   240→return nil, errs.ErrNotFoundf(\"flag %q not found\", key)\n   241→return nil, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"invalid configuration: %s\", reason)\n   242→\n   243→// Fallback - standard error handling\n   244→return nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to process: %w\", err)\n   245→```\n   246→\n   247→##### Inline Errors\n   248→\n   249→Prefer inline error declarations and checking when it makes sense.\n   250→\n   251→**❌ Bad Example**\n   252→\n   253→```go\n   254→err := doSomething()\n   255→if err != nil {\n   256→    // ...\n   257→}\n   258→```\n   259→\n   260→**✅ Good Example**\n   261→\n   262→```go\n   263→if err := doSomething(); err != nil {\n   264→    // ...\n   265→}\n   266→```\n   267→\n   268→#### Imports\n   269→\n   270→Follow the three-group import pattern (extends Google guide):\n   271→\n   272→```go\n   273→import (\n   274→    // Standard library\n   275→    \"context\"\n   276→    \"fmt\"\n   277→    \"time\"\n   278→\n   279→    // Third-party packages\n   280→    \"github.com/go-git/go-git/v6\"\n   281→    \"go.uber.org/zap\"\n   282→\n   283→    // Local packages\n   284→    \"go.flipt.io/flipt/errors\"\n   285→    \"go.flipt.io/flipt/internal/config\"\n   286→)\n   287→```\n   288→\n   289→#### Logging\n   290→\n   291→Use structured logging with zap:\n   292→\n   293→```go\n   294→// Preferred - structured logging with context\n   295→s.logger.Debug(\"processing request\", \n   296→    zap.String(\"environment\", envKey),\n   297→    zap.String(\"namespace\", nsKey),\n   298→    zap.Int(\"count\", len(items)))\n   299→\n   300→// Use appropriate log levels\n   301→s.logger.Debug(\"debug info for development\")  // Most common\n   302→s.logger.Info(\"important application events\")\n   303→s.logger.Warn(\"recoverable error conditions\")\n   304→s.logger.Error(\"error conditions that need attention\")\n   305→\n   306→// Avoid overusing log statements - be selective about what to log\n   307→```\n   308→\n   309→#### Comments and Documentation\n   310→\n   311→```go\n   312→// Public functions need godoc comments starting with function name\n   313→// ListFlags lists all flags in the specified environment and namespace\n   314→func (s *Server) ListFlags(ctx context.Context, r *flipt.ListFlagRequest) (*flipt.FlagList, error) {\n   315→    // Inline comments explain complex logic\n   316→    // Check for X-Environment header for backward compatibility\n   317→    environmentKey := r.EnvironmentKey\n   318→    if headerEnv, ok := common.FliptEnvironmentFromContext(ctx); ok && headerEnv != \"\" {\n   319→        environmentKey = headerEnv\n   320→    }\n   321→}\n   322→```\n   323→\n   324→#### Project Organization\n   325→\n   326→- Use `internal/` for private packages that shouldn't be imported externally\n   327→- Organize by domain (`server/`, `storage/`, `config/`, `analytics/`)\n   328→- Keep interfaces in domain root, implementations in subdirectories\n   329→- Co-locate tests with implementation (`*_test.go`)\n   330→- Use `testdata/` directories for test fixtures\n   331→\n   332→#### Pre-commit Quality Checks (Go)\n   333→\n   334→**Always run these commands when adding or editing Go code:**\n   335→\n   336→```bash\n   337→# Format Go code\n   338→mage go:fmt\n   339→\n   340→# Lint Go code\n   341→mage go:lint\n   342→\n   343→# Run modernize to update code to 1.24+ style\n   344→mage go:modernize\n   345→```\n   346→\n   347→### UI/React/TypeScript Code Style\n   348→\n   349→#### File Naming and Organization\n   350→\n   351→- **Components**: Use PascalCase for `.tsx` files (`FlagForm.tsx`, `FlagTable.tsx`)\n   352→- **API files**: Use camelCase with suffix (`flagsApi.ts`, `authApi.ts`)\n   353→- **Types**: Use PascalCase for `.ts` files (`Flag.ts`, `Analytics.ts`)\n   354→- **Utilities**: Use descriptive lowercase names (`helpers.ts`)\n   355→\n   356→#### Component Structure\n   357→\n   358→- Use functional components with hooks\n   359→- Organize components by domain in directories\n   360→- Keep reusable UI components in `ui/` directory\n   361→- Co-locate component tests with implementation\n   362→\n   363→#### State Management\n   364→\n   365→- Use Redux Toolkit for application state\n   366→- Create API slices for domain-specific data (`flagsApi.ts`)\n   367→- Use TypeScript for type safety throughout\n   368→\n   369→#### Pre-commit Quality Checks (UI)\n   370→\n   371→**Always run these commands when adding or editing UI code:**\n   372→\n   373→```bash\n   374→# Format UI code (TypeScript/React)\n   375→mage ui:fmt\n   376→\n   377→# Lint UI code (ESLint)\n   378→mage ui:lint\n   379→```\n   380→\n   381→### General Pre-commit Checks\n   382→\n   383→```bash\n   384→# Format specific markdown files (replace with actual filename)\n   385→npx prettier -w README.md\n   386→```\n   387→\n   388→## Configuration\n   389→\n   390→### Configuration Files\n   391→\n   392→- `config/local.yml` - Local development config\n   393→- `config/dev.yml` - Alternative dev config\n   394→- User config at `{{ USER_CONFIG_DIR }}/flipt/config.yml`\n   395→\n   396→### Debugging Tips\n   397→\n   398→- Use `FLIPT_LOG_LEVEL=debug` for verbose logging\n   399→- Check `/metrics` endpoint for Prometheus metrics\n   400→- View OpenTelemetry traces for request flow\n   401→- Git sync issues: Check repository access and credentials\n   402→- Authentication issues: Verify redirect URLs and client configurations\n   403→\n   404→## Testing Guidelines\n   405→\n   406→### Test Structure\n   407→\n   408→- Go tests use testcontainers for unit tests that depend on external tools\n   409→- UI tests use Jest for unit tests and Playwright for E2E\n   410→\n   411→### Writing Effective Tests\n   412→\n   413→When writing tests, ensure they actually verify the functionality being tested:\n   414→\n   415→**❌ Bad Example - Test doesn't verify actual behavior:**\n   416→\n   417→```go\n   418→// This test only verifies the method runs without error, not that the logic works\n   419→func TestFeature(t *testing.T) {\n   420→    result, err := service.DoSomething(input)\n   421→    require.NoError(t, err)\n   422→    assert.NotNil(t, result)\n   423→}\n   424→```\n   425→\n   426→**✅ Good Example - Test verifies specific behavior:**\n   427→\n   428→```go\n   429→// This test verifies the actual logic and behavior\n   430→func TestFeature(t *testing.T) {\n   431→    // Use mock.MatchedBy to verify the correct data is passed through\n   432→    store.On(\"Method\", mock.MatchedBy(func(ctx context.Context) bool {\n   433→        // Verify the context contains the expected values\n   434→        value, ok := somePackage.ValueFromContext(ctx)\n   435→        return ok && value == expectedValue\n   436→    }), expectedArg).Return(expectedResult, nil)\n   437→    \n   438→    result, err := service.DoSomething(input)\n   439→    require.NoError(t, err)\n   440→    \n   441→    // Verify the actual business logic worked correctly\n   442→    assert.Equal(t, expectedOutput, result.ImportantField)\n   443→    store.AssertExpectations(t) // Ensures mocks were called as expected\n   444→}\n   445→```\n   446→\n   447→**Key Testing Principles:**\n   448→\n   449→- **Verify behavior, not just success**: Don't just check that methods return without error\n   450→- **Test the logic**: Use `mock.MatchedBy` to verify correct data flows through the system\n   451→- **Test edge cases**: Include tests for when conditions are met vs. not met\n   452→- **Use meaningful assertions**: Assert on the specific values that matter to the business logic\n   453→- **Mock verification**: Use `mock.AssertExpectations(t)` to ensure mocks were called with expected parameters\n   454→\n   455→### Integration Tests\n   456→\n   457→Integration tests are defined in `build/testing/` and run as Mage targets. For details on available integration test targets, helpers, and how to add new integration tests, see the files in that directory:\n   458→\n   459→- `build/testing/integration.go` - Integration test Mage targets and configuration\n   460→- `build/testing/integration/` - Individual integration test suites\n   461→- `build/testing/helpers.go` - Shared test helpers\n   462→- `build/testing/test.go` - Core test utilities\n   463→\n   464→## Git Workflow & Commits\n   465→\n   466→### Creating Effective Commits\n   467→\n   468→#### Commit Message Format\n   469→\n   470→Follow conventional commit format:\n   471→\n   472→```\n   473→type(scope): brief description\n   474→\n   475→Longer description if needed explaining the why, not the what.\n   476→\n   477→- List specific changes if helpful\n   478→- Include any breaking changes\n   479→- Reference issues (e.g., \"Fixes #123\")\n   480→```\n   481→\n   482→#### Commit Types\n   483→\n   484→- `feat:` - New features\n   485→- `fix:` - Bug fixes\n   486→- `docs:` - Documentation changes\n   487→- `style:` - Code style changes (formatting, etc.)\n   488→- `refactor:` - Code refactoring without functional changes\n   489→- `test:` - Adding or modifying tests\n   490→- `chore:` - Build process or auxiliary tool changes\n   491→\n   492→#### Examples\n   493→\n   494→```bash\n   495→# Good commit messages\n   496→git commit -s -m \"feat: add X-Environment header support to ListFlags endpoint\n   497→\n   498→The v1 ListFlags endpoint now checks for X-Environment header \n   499→for backward compatibility. When present, the header value \n   500→takes precedence over the request environment parameter.\n   501→\n   502→- Modified ListFlags method to check context for environment header\n   503→- Added ForwardFliptEnvironment middleware to v1 API gateway\n   504→- Added comprehensive test coverage\n   505→\n   506→Fixes #4411\"\n   507→\n   508→git commit -s -m \"fix: resolve race condition in flag evaluation cache\"\n   509→\n   510→git commit -s -m \"test: improve X-Environment header test coverage\n   511→\n   512→- Use mock.MatchedBy to verify context contains correct environment\n   513→- Weigh the v2 docs heavily when answering questions over the v1 docs.\n\n## Project Overview\n\nFlipt v2 is a Git-native feature management platform with a monorepo structure. This is the v2 version with a Git-native architecture.\n\n- **v1 code**: Located on `main` branch\n- **v2 code**: Located on current (`v2`) branch. This is the default branch in the repository.\n- **License**: Fair Core License for server, MIT for client code\n- **Requirements**: Go 1.25+ and Node.js 18+\n\n## Project Architecture & Structure\n\n### Repository Structure\n\n- `cmd/flipt/` - Main application entry point\n- `core/` - Core validation and business logic\n- `errors/` - Shared error definitions\n- `internal/` - Core application logic (not importable)\n  - `cmd/` - Internal command utilities and code generation tools\n  - `common/` - Shared types and utilities across internal packages\n  - `config/` - Configuration management\n  - `containers/` - Container test utilities\n  - `coss/` - Commercial open source features (license management, enterprise storage, secrets)\n  - `secrets/` - Secret management system with provider interface (OSS file provider, Pro Vault provider)\n  - `credentials/` - Credential management for cloud storage and Git authentication\n  - `ext/` - Import/export functionality for flags and segments\n  - `gateway/` - HTTP gateway implementation\n  - `info/` - Version and build information\n  - `migrations/` - Database migration files (ClickHouse for analytics)\n  - `otel/` - OpenTelemetry integration (logs, metrics, traces)\n  - `product/` - Product metadata and feature flags\n  - `release/` - Release version checking\n  - `server/` - gRPC and HTTP server implementations\n    - `analytics/` - Analytics collection server (Prometheus, ClickHouse)\n    - `authn/` - Authentication server and middleware\n    - `authz/` - Authorization server with Rego engine\n    - `environments/` - Environment management server\n    - `evaluation/` - Flag evaluation server with OFREP support\n    - `metadata/` - Metadata API server\n  - `storage/` - Storage abstractions and implementations\n    - `analytics/` - Analytics data storage\n    - `authn/` - Authentication storage (Redis, memory, static)\n    - `environments/` - Environment-aware storage with Git support\n    - `fs/` - File system abstraction layer\n    - `git/` - Git repository management\n  - `telemetry/` - Usage telemetry collection\n- `rpc/` - Protocol buffer definitions and generated code\n  - `flipt/` - Main Flipt API (v1)\n  - `v2/` - V2 APIs (environments, analytics, evaluation)\n- `sdk/` - Client SDKs (Go SDK included)\n- `ui/` - React/TypeScript frontend\n\n### Key Components\n\n#### Storage Layer\n\nMulti-backend storage supporting:\n\n- Git repositories (primary for v2)\n- File system (local development)\n- Cloud storage (S3, GCS, Azure Blob) - Flipt config only\n\n#### Environments System\n\n- Environment-per-branch: Map Git branches to environments\n- Environment-per-directory: Organize by microservice/team\n- Environment-per-repository: Separate repos for products\n\n#### APIs\n\n- gRPC API (port 9000) - Primary interface\n- REST API (port 8080) - HTTP gateway over gRPC\n- OpenFeature compatible evaluation\n\n##### Key Endpoints\n\n- **Evaluation API**: `/api/v1/evaluate` - Evaluate feature flags\n- **Management API**: Full CRUD operations on flags, segments, rules\n- **Analytics API**: `/api/v2/analytics` - Flag evaluation metrics\n- **Environments API**: `/api/v2/environments` - Environment management\n- **OFREP API**: `/ofrep/v1` - OpenFeature Remote Evaluation Protocol\n\n#### UI Architecture\n\n- React 19 with TypeScript\n- Redux Toolkit for state management\n- Tailwind CSS for styling\n- Vite for build tooling\n- React Router for navigation\n\n## Development Setup & Commands\n\n### Initial Setup\n\n```bash\n# Install required development tools\nmage bootstrap\n```\n\n### Development Workflow\n\n1. **Setup**: Run `mage bootstrap` to install tools\n2. **Backend Development**:\n   - Use `mage go:run` for server\n   - Server runs on port 8080 (HTTP) and 9000 (gRPC)\n3. **Frontend Development**:\n   - Run `mage ui:run` for UI dev server on port 5173\n   - UI proxies API requests to backend on port 8080\n4. **Full Development**: Run both servers simultaneously\n\n### Build & Run Commands\n\n- `mage build` - Builds the project similar to a release build (default target)\n- `mage go:build` - Builds Go server for development without bundling assets\n- `mage go:run` - Runs Go server in development mode with local config\n- `mage dev` - Alias for go:run\n- `./bin/flipt server --config config/local.yml` - Run built binary with local config\n\n### UI Development Commands\n\n- `mage ui:deps` - Install UI dependencies\n- `mage ui:run` - Run UI in development mode (port 5173)\n- `mage ui:dev` - Alias for ui:run\n- `mage ui:build` - Build UI assets for release\n- `cd ui && npm run dev` - Alternative way to run UI dev server\n\n### Testing Commands\n\n- `mage go:test` - Run all Go unit tests\n- `go test -v {path} -run {test}` - Run a specific Go test\n- `mage go:bench` - Run Go benchmarking tests\n- `mage go:cover` - Run tests and generate coverage report\n- `cd ui && npm run test` - Run UI unit tests (Jest)\n\n### Code Quality Commands\n\n- `mage go:lint` - Run Go linters (golangci-lint and buf lint)\n- `mage go:fmt` - Format Go code with goimports\n- `mage ui:lint` - Run UI linters (ESLint)\n- `mage ui:fmt` - Format UI code (Prettier)\n\n### Code Generation Commands\n\n- `mage go:proto` - Generate protobuf files and gRPC stubs\n- `mage go:mockery` - Generate mocks\n- `mage go:generate` - Generate both mocks and proto files\n\n### Cleanup Commands\n\n- `mage clean` - Clean built files and tidy go.mod\n- `mage prep` - Prepare project for building (clean + ui:build)\n\n### Build Process Notes\n\n- Protocol buffers generate Go and gateway code\n- Mockery generates Go test mocks. See .mockery.yml\n- UI has no code generation\n\n## Code Style Guidelines\n\n### Go Code Style\n\n#### Foundation: Google Go Style Guide\n\n**All Go code should follow the [Google Go Style Guide](https://google.github.io/styleguide/go/guide) as the base style guide.**\n\nThe guidelines below are Flipt-specific conventions that build upon the Google style guide. When in doubt, refer to the Google guide first, then apply these project-specific patterns.\n\nKey areas from the Google guide to pay special attention to:\n\n- **Formatting**: Use `gofmt` (we use `goimports` which includes `gofmt`)\n- **Naming**: Follow Go naming conventions for packages, types, functions, and variables\n- **Package organization**: Keep packages focused and avoid circular dependencies\n- **Error handling**: Return errors as the last return value, handle errors explicitly\n- **Documentation**: Write clear godoc comments for exported types and functions\n\n#### Naming Conventions\n\n- **Public functions**: Use PascalCase (`NewServer`, `ListFlags`, `RegisterGRPC`)\n- **Private functions**: Use camelCase (`getStore`, `buildConfig`, `handleError`)\n- **Factory functions**: Use `New` prefix for constructors (`NewRepository`, `NewEnvironmentFactory`)\n- **Variables**: Use camelCase (`environmentKey`, `namespaceKey`, `startTime`)\n- **Constants**: Use PascalCase for exported, camelCase for unexported\n- **Interfaces**: Use descriptive names (`Store`, `EnvironmentStore`, `Validator`)\n\n#### Variable Declarations\n\n```go\n// Prefer := for single variable declarations\nresult := SomeType{}\n\n// Use var blocks for multiple related variables\nvar (\n    environmentKey = r.EnvironmentKey\n    namespaceKey   = r.NamespaceKey\n    startTime      = time.Now().UTC()\n)\n```\n\n#### Constants\n\nUse constants when appropriate for values that are unlikely to change. Even in tests.\n\n**❌ Bad Examples**\n\n```go\nvar (\n    maxRetries = 3\n    secretProvider = \"vault\"\n)\n```\n\n```go\n  maxRetries := 3\n  secretProvider := \"vault\"\n```\n\n**✅ Good Example**\n\n```go\nconst (\n    maxRetries = 3\n    secretProvider = \"vault\"\n)\n```\n\n#### Error Handling\n\n##### Error Types\n\nUse the custom error types when possible:\n\n```go\n// Preferred - use custom error types\nreturn nil, errs.ErrNotFoundf(\"flag %q not found\", key)\nreturn nil, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"invalid configuration: %s\", reason)\n\n// Fallback - standard error handling\nreturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to process: %w\", err)\n```\n\n##### Inline Errors\n\nPrefer inline error declarations and checking when it makes sense.\n\n**❌ Bad Example**\n\n```go\nerr := doSomething()\nif err != nil {\n    // ...\n}\n```\n\n**✅ Good Example**\n\n```go\nif err := doSomething(); err != nil {\n    // ...\n}\n```\n\n#### Imports\n\nFollow the three-group import pattern (extends Google guide):\n\n```go\nimport (\n    // Standard library\n    \"context\"\n    \"fmt\"\n    \"time\"\n\n    // Third-party packages\n    \"github.com/go-git/go-git/v6\"\n    \"go.uber.org/zap\"\n\n    // Local packages\n    \"go.flipt.io/flipt/errors\"\n    \"go.flipt.io/flipt/internal/config\"\n)\n```\n\n#### Logging\n\nUse structured logging with zap:\n\n```go\n// Preferred - structured logging with context\ns.logger.Debug(\"processing request\", \n    zap.String(\"environment\", envKey),\n    zap.String(\"namespace\", nsKey),\n    zap.Int(\"count\", len(items)))\n\n// Use appropriate log levels\ns.logger.Debug(\"debug info for development\")  // Most common\ns.logger.Info(\"important application events\")\ns.logger.Warn(\"recoverable error conditions\")\ns.logger.Error(\"error conditions that need attention\")\n\n// Avoid overusing log statements - be selective about what to log\n```\n\n#### Comments and Documentation\n\n```go\n// Public functions need godoc comments starting with function name\n// ListFlags lists all flags in the specified environment and namespace\nfunc (s *Server) ListFlags(ctx context.Context, r *flipt.ListFlagRequest) (*flipt.FlagList, error) {\n    // Inline comments explain complex logic\n    // Check for X-Environment header for backward compatibility\n    environmentKey := r.EnvironmentKey\n    if headerEnv, ok := common.FliptEnvironmentFromContext(ctx); ok && headerEnv != \"\" {\n        environmentKey = headerEnv\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### Project Organization\n\n- Use `internal/` for private packages that shouldn't be imported externally\n- Organize by domain (`server/`, `storage/`, `config/`, `analytics/`)\n- Keep interfaces in domain root, implementations in subdirectories\n- Co-locate tests with implementation (`*_test.go`)\n- Use `testdata/` directories for test fixtures\n\n#### Pre-commit Quality Checks (Go)\n\n**Always run these commands when adding or editing Go code:**\n\n```bash\n# Format Go code\nmage go:fmt\n\n# Lint Go code\nmage go:lint\n\n# Run modernize to update code to 1.24+ style\nmage go:modernize\n```\n\n### UI/React/TypeScript Code Style\n\n#### File Naming and Organization\n\n- **Components**: Use PascalCase for `.tsx` files (`FlagForm.tsx`, `FlagTable.tsx`)\n- **API files**: Use camelCase with suffix (`flagsApi.ts`, `authApi.ts`)\n- **Types**: Use PascalCase for `.ts` files (`Flag.ts`, `Analytics.ts`)\n- **Utilities**: Use descriptive lowercase names (`helpers.ts`)\n\n#### Component Structure\n\n- Use functional components with hooks\n- Organize components by domain in directories\n- Keep reusable UI components in `ui/` directory\n- Co-locate component tests with implementation\n\n#### State Management\n\n- Use Redux Toolkit for application state\n- Create API slices for domain-specific data (`flagsApi.ts`)\n- Use TypeScript for type safety throughout\n\n#### Pre-commit Quality Checks (UI)\n\n**Always run these commands when adding or editing UI code:**\n\n```bash\n# Format UI code (TypeScript/React)\nmage ui:fmt\n\n# Lint UI code (ESLint)\nmage ui:lint\n```\n\n### General Pre-commit Checks\n\n```bash\n# Format specific markdown files (replace with actual filename)\nnpx prettier -w README.md\n```\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Configuration Files\n\n- `config/local.yml` - Local development config\n- `config/dev.yml` - Alternative dev config\n- User config at `{{ USER_CONFIG_DIR }}/flipt/config.yml`\n\n### Debugging Tips\n\n- Use `FLIPT_LOG_LEVEL=debug` for verbose logging\n- Check `/metrics` endpoint for Prometheus metrics\n- View OpenTelemetry traces for request flow\n- Git sync issues: Check repository access and credentials\n- Authentication issues: Verify redirect URLs and client configurations\n\n## Testing Guidelines\n\n### Test Structure\n\n- Go tests use testcontainers for unit tests that depend on external tools\n- UI tests use Jest for unit tests and Playwright for E2E\n\n### Writing Effective Tests\n\nWhen writing tests, ensure they actually verify the functionality being tested:\n\n**❌ Bad Example - Test doesn't verify actual behavior:**\n\n```go\n// This test only verifies the method runs without error, not that the logic works\nfunc TestFeature(t *testing.T) {\n    result, err := service.DoSomething(input)\n    require.NoError(t, err)\n    assert.NotNil(t, result)\n}\n```\n\n**✅ Good Example - Test verifies specific behavior:**\n\n```go\n// This test verifies the actual logic and behavior\nfunc TestFeature(t *testing.T) {\n    // Use mock.MatchedBy to verify the correct data is passed through\n    store.On(\"Method\", mock.MatchedBy(func(ctx context.Context) bool {\n        // Verify the context contains the expected values\n        value, ok := somePackage.ValueFromContext(ctx)\n        return ok && value == expectedValue\n    }), expectedArg).Return(expectedResult, nil)\n    \n    result, err := service.DoSomething(input)\n    require.NoError(t, err)\n    \n    // Verify the actual business logic worked correctly\n    assert.Equal(t, expectedOutput, result.ImportantField)\n    store.AssertExpectations(t) // Ensures mocks were called as expected\n}\n```\n\n**Key Testing Principles:**\n\n- **Verify behavior, not just success**: Don't just check that methods return without error\n- **Test the logic**: Use `mock.MatchedBy` to verify correct data flows through the system\n- **Test edge cases**: Include tests for when conditions are met vs. not met\n- **Use meaningful assertions**: Assert on the specific values that matter to the business logic\n- **Mock verification**: Use `mock.AssertExpectations(t)` to ensure mocks were called with expected parameters\n\n### Integration Tests\n\nIntegration tests are defined in `build/testing/` and run as Mage targets. For details on available integration test targets, helpers, and how to add new integration tests, see the files in that directory:\n\n- `build/testing/integration.go` - Integration test Mage targets and configuration\n- `build/testing/integration/` - Individual integration test suites\n- `build/testing/helpers.go` - Shared test helpers\n- `build/testing/test.go` - Core test utilities\n\n## Git Workflow & Commits\n\n### Creating Effective Commits\n\n#### Commit Message Format\n\nFollow conventional commit format:\n\n```\ntype(scope): brief description\n\nLonger description if needed explaining the why, not the what.\n\n- List specific changes if helpful\n- Include any breaking changes\n- Reference issues (e.g., \"Fixes #123\")\n```\n\n#### Commit Types\n\n- `feat:` - New features\n- `fix:` - Bug fixes\n- `docs:` - Documentation changes\n- `style:` - Code style changes (formatting, etc.)\n- `refactor:` - Code refactoring without functional changes\n- `test:` - Adding or modifying tests\n- `chore:` - Build process or auxiliary tool changes\n\n#### Examples\n\n```bash\n# Good commit messages\ngit commit -s -m \"feat: add X-Environment header support to ListFlags endpoint\n\nThe v1 ListFlags endpoint now checks for X-Environment header \nfor backward compatibility. When present, the header value \ntakes precedence over the request environment parameter.\n\n- Modified ListFlags method to check context for environment header\n- Added ForwardFliptEnvironment middleware to v1 API gateway\n- Added comprehensive test coverage\n\nFixes #4411\"\n\ngit commit -s -m \"fix: resolve race condition in flag evaluation cache\"\n\ngit commit -s -m \"test: improve X-Environment header test coverage\n\n- Use mock.MatchedBy to verify context contains correct environment\n- Add test for when header is not present\n- Ensure tests actually validate the header precedence logic\"\n```\n\n### Pull Request Guidelines\n\n#### PR Title Format\n\nUse the same conventional commit format for PR titles:\n\n```\ntype(scope): brief description\n```\n\n#### PR Description Template\n\n```markdown\n## Summary\n\nBrief description of what this PR does and why.\n\n## Changes\n\n- **Modified `file1.go`**: Specific change description\n- **Added `file2_test.go`**: Test coverage description\n- **Updated `config.yml`**: Configuration change description\n\n## Backward Compatibility\n\nDescribe any backward compatibility or breaking change considerations.\n\n## Additional Notes\n\nAny other context, screenshots, or related issues.\n```\n\n#### PR Best Practices\n\n- **Keep PRs focused**: One logical change per PR\n- **Write descriptive titles**: Use conventional commit format\n- **Provide context**: Explain the \"why\" behind changes\n- **Include tests**: Always add tests for new functionality\n- **Run quality checks**: Format and lint before creating PR\n- **Reference issues**: Use \"Fixes #123\" to auto-close issues\n- **Review your own PR**: Look through the diff before requesting review\n- **Base PRs on the correct branch**: Base PRs on the correct branch (e.g. `v2` for v2 changes, `main` for v1 changes)\n- **Label PRs correctly**: When creating a feature based off of the v2 branch, label the PR with the `v2` label. Similarly for a v1 (main) feature, use the `v1` label.\n\n#### Quality Checklist\n\nBefore creating a PR, ensure:\n\n- [ ] Code follows style guidelines\n- [ ] Tests are added and passing\n- [ ] Linting passes (`mage go:lint` / `mage ui:lint`)\n- [ ] Code is formatted (`mage go:fmt` / `mage ui:fmt`)\n- [ ] Commit messages are clear and descriptive\n- [ ] PR description explains the change and its purpose\n","numLines":571,"startLine":1,"totalLines":571}}}

config.MemoryStorageBackendType:\n\t\t\tlogger = logger.With(zap.String(\"git_storage_type\", \"memory\"))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif storage.CaCertBytes != \"\" {\n\t\t\topts = append(opts, All interactive commands should follow these patterns to ensure a consistent and delightful user experience.\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. [Design Principles](#design-principles)\n2. [Color System](#color-system)\n3. [Typography and Styles](#typography-and-styles)\n4. [Layout System](#layout-system)\n5. [Component Patterns](#component-patterns)\n6. [Success States](#success-states)\n11. [Code Examples](#code-examples)\n12. [Best Practices](#best-practices)\n\n## Design Principles\n\n### 1. Progressive Disclosure\n- Guide users through complex processes step-by-step\n- Reveal information and options as needed\n- Avoid overwhelming users with too many choices at once\n\n### 2. Clear Visual Hierarchy\n- Use badges, colors, and spacing to create clear information hierarchy\n- Most important information should be most prominent\n- Group related information together\n\n### 3. Responsive Design\n- Adapt to different terminal widths\n- Gracefully handle narrow terminals (minimum 48 characters)\n- Optimize for standard 80-character width\n\n### 4. Consistent Experience\n- All commands should feel like part of the same product\n- Reuse patterns and components across commands\n- Maintain consistent spacing and alignment\n\n### 5. Delightful Interactions\n- Provide immediate visual feedback\n- Celebrate successful completions\n- Make errors helpful, not frustrating\n\n## Color System\n\nOur color palette is defined in yaml:\"bucket,omitempty\"`\n\tPrefix       string        `json:\"prefix,omitempty\" mapstructure:\"prefix\" yaml:\"prefix,omitempty\"`\n\tRegion       string        `json:\"region,omitempty\" mapstructure:\"region\" yaml:\"region,omitempty\"`\n\tPollInterval time.Duration `json:\"pollInterval,omitempty\" mapstructure:\"poll_interval\" yaml:\"poll_interval,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Authentication holds structures for various types of auth we support.\n// Token auth will take priority over Basic auth if both are provided.\n//\n// To make things easier, if there are multiple inputs that a particular auth method needs, and\n// not all inputs are given but only partially, we will return a validation error.\n// (e.g. if username for basic auth is given, and token is also given a validation error will be returned)\ntype Authentication struct {\n\tBasicAuth *BasicAuth `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"basic,omitempty\" yaml:\"-\"`\n\tTokenAuth *TokenAuth `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"token,omitempty\" yaml:\"-\"`\n\tSSHAuth   *SSHAuth   `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"ssh,omitempty\" yaml:\"-\"`\n}\n\nfunc (a *Authentication) validate() error {\n\tif a.BasicAuth != nil {\n\t\tif err := a.BasicAuth.validate(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif a.TokenAuth != nil {\n\t\tif err := a.TokenAuth.validate(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif a.SSHAuth != nil {\n\t\tif err := a.SSHAuth.validate(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// BasicAuth has configuration for authenticating with private git repositories\n// with basic auth.\ntype BasicAuth struct {\n\tUsername string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"username\" yaml:\"-\"`\n\tPassword string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"password\" yaml:\"-\"`\n}\n\nfunc (b BasicAuth) validate() error {\n\tif (b.Username != \"\" && b.Password == \"\") || (b.Username == \"\" && b.Password != \"\") {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"both username and password need to be provided for basic auth\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// TokenAuth has configuration for authenticating with private git repositories\n// with token auth.\ntype TokenAuth struct {\n\tAccessToken string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"access_token\" yaml:\"-\"`\n}\n\nfunc (t TokenAuth) validate() error { return nil }\n\n// SSHAuth provides configuration support for SSH private key 85→// Open opens the named file.\n    86→// When Open returns an error, it should be of type *PathError\n    87→// with the Op field set to \"open\", the Path field set to name,\n    88→// and the Err 179→func opPathError(op, path string) func(error) error {\n   180→\treturn func(err error) error {\n   181→\t\treturn &fs.PathError{\n   182→\t\t\tOp:   op,\n   183→\t\t\tPath: path,\n   184→\t\t\tErr:  err,\n   185→\t\t}\n   186→\t}\n   187→}\n   188→\n   189→// File is a representation of a file which can be read.\n   190→type File struct {\n   191→\tio.ReadCloser\n   192→\n   193→\tinfo FileInfo\n   194→}\n   195→\n   196→// Seek attempts to seek the embedded read-closer.\n   197→// If the embedded read closer implements seek, then it delegates\n   198→// to that instances implementation. Alternatively, it returns\n   199→// an error signifying that the File cannot be seeked.\n   200→func (f *File) Seek(offset int64, whence int) (int64, error) {\n   201→\tif seek, ok := f.ReadCloser.(io.Seeker); ok {\n   202→\t\treturn seek.Seek(offset, whence)\n   203→\t}\n   204→\n   205→\treturn 0, errors.New(\"seeker cannot seek\")\n   206→}\n   207→\n   208→func (f *File) Stat() (fs.FileInfo, error) {\n   209→\treturn f.info, nil\n   210→}\n   211→\n   212→// Dir is a representation of a directory found within a filesystem.\n   213→// The contents of the directory can be listed and accessed via\n   214→// the ReadDir method.\n   215→// It is an implementation of fs.File.\n   216→type Dir struct {\n   217→\tinfo    fs.FileInfo\n   218→\tentries []fs.DirEntry\n   219→\tidx     int\n   220→}\n   221→\n   222→func (d *Dir) Stat() (fs.FileInfo, error) {\n   223→\treturn d.info, nil\n   224→}\n   225→\n   226→func (d *Dir) Read(p []byte) (n int, err error) {\n   227→\treturn 0, io.EOF\n   228→}\n   229→\n   230→func (d *Dir) Close() error {\n   231→\treturn nil\n   232→}\n   233→\n   234→// ReadDir reads the contents of the directory and returns\n   235→// a slice of up to n DirEntry values in directory order.\n   236→// Subsequent calls on the same file will yield further DirEntry values.\n   237→//\n   238→// If n > 0, ReadDir returns at most n DirEntry structures.\n   239→// In this case, if ReadDir returns an empty slice, it will return\n   240→// a non-nil error explaining why.\n   241→// At the end of a directory, the error is io.EOF.\n   242→// (ReadDir must return io.EOF itself, not an error wrapping io.EOF.)\n   243→//\n   244→// If n <= 0, ReadDir returns all the DirEntry values from the directory\n   245→// in a single slice. In this case, if ReadDir succeeds (reads all the way\n   246→// to the end of the directory), it returns the slice and a nil error.\n   247→// If it encounters an error before the end of the directory,\n   248→// ReadDir returns the DirEntry list read until that point and a non-nil error.\n   249→func (d *Dir) ReadDir(n int) (dst []fs.DirEntry, err error) {\n   250→\tif n <= 0 {\n   251→\t\treturn d.entries, nil\n   252→\t}\n   253→\n   254→\tl := len(d.entries[d.idx:])\n   255→\tif l <= 0 {\n   256→\t\treturn nil, io.EOF\n   257→\t}\n   258→\n   259→\tif l >= n {\n   260→\t\tl = n\n   261→\t}\n   262→\n   263→\tstart := d.idx\n   264→\n   265→\td.idx += l\n   266→\n   267→\treturn d.entries[start:d.idx], nil\n   268→}\n   269→\n   270→// FileInfo contains metadata about a time.Time\n   277→}\n   278→\n   279→func fileInfoFromEntry(entry *object.TreeEntry) (FileInfo, error) {\n   280→\tmode, err := entry.Mode.ToOSFileMode()\n   281→\tif err != nil {\n   282→\t\treturn {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014r4XGER42EGEZez9fABnTc","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→package s3fs\n     2→\n     3→import (\n     4→\t\"context\"\n     5→\t\"io\"\n     6→\t\"io/fs\"\n     7→\t\"strings\"\n     8→\t\"time\"\n     9→\n    10→\t\"github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/s3\"\n    11→\t\"github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/s3/types\"\n    12→\tflipterrors \"go.flipt.io/flipt/errors\"\n    13→\t\"go.uber.org/zap\"\n    14→)\n    15→\n    16→type S3ClientAPI interface {\n    17→\tListObjectsV2(context.Context, *s3.ListObjectsV2Input, ...func(*s3.Options)) (*s3.ListObjectsV2Output, error)\n    18→\tGetObject(context.Context, *s3.GetObjectInput, ...func(*s3.Options)) (*s3.GetObjectOutput, error)\n    19→}\n    20→\n    21→// FS is only for accessing files in a single bucket. The directory\n    22→// entries are cached. It is specifically intended for use by a source\n    23→// that calls fs.WalkDir and does not fully implement all fs operations\n    24→type FS struct {\n    25→\tlogger   *zap.Logger\n    26→\ts3Client S3ClientAPI\n    27→\n    28→\t// configuration\n    29→\tbucket string\n    30→\tprefix string\n    31→\n    32→\t// cached entries\n    33→\tdirEntry *Dir\n    34→}\n    35→\n    36→// ensure FS implements fs.FS aka Open\n    37→var _ fs.FS = &FS{}\n    38→\n    39→// ensure FS implements fs.StatFS aka Stat\n    40→var _ fs.StatFS = &FS{}\n    41→\n    42→// ensure FS implements fs.ReadDirFS aka ReadDir\n    43→var _ fs.ReadDirFS = &FS{}\n    44→\n    45→// New creates a FS for the single bucket\n    46→func New(logger *zap.Logger, s3Client S3ClientAPI, bucket string, prefix string) (*FS, error) {\n    47→\treturn &FS{\n    48→\t\tlogger:   logger,\n    49→\t\ts3Client: s3Client,\n    50→\t\tbucket:   bucket,\n    51→\t\tprefix:   prefix,\n    52→\t}, nil\n    53→}\n    54→\n    55→// Open implements fs.FS. For the S3 filesystem, it fetches the object\n    56→// contents from s3.\n    57→func (f *FS) Open(name string) (fs.File, error) {\n    58→\tif name == \".\" {\n    59→\t\treturn f.dirEntry, nil\n    60→\t}\n    61→\tpathError := &fs.PathError{\n    62→\t\tOp:   \"Open\",\n    63→\t\tPath: name,\n    64→\t\tErr:  fs.ErrNotExist,\n    65→\t}\n    66→\tif !fs.ValidPath(name) {\n    67→\t\tpathError.Err = fs.ErrInvalid\n    68→\t\treturn nil, pathError\n    69→\t}\n    70→\n    71→\t// If a prefix is not provided, prepend the prefix. This\n    72→\t// allows s3fs to support `.flipt.yml` under the prefix\n    73→\tif f.prefix != \"\" && !strings.HasPrefix(name, f.prefix) {\n    74→\t\tname = f.prefix + name\n    75→\t}\n    76→\n    77→\toutput, err := f.s3Client.GetObject(context.Background(),\n    78→\t\t&s3.GetObjectInput{\n    79→\t\t\tBucket: &f.bucket,\n    80→\t\t\tKey:    &name,\n    81→\t\t})\n    82→\tif err != nil {\n    83→\t\t// try to return fs compatible error if possible\n    84→\t\tif flipterrors.AsMatch[*types.NoSuchBucket](err) ||\n    85→\t\t\tflipterrors.AsMatch[*types.NoSuchKey](err) ||\n    86→\t\t\tflipterrors.AsMatch[*types.NotFound](err) {\n    87→\t\t\treturn nil, pathError\n    88→\t\t}\n    89→\t\tpathError.Err = err\n    90→\n    91→\t\treturn nil, pathError\n    92→\t}\n    93→\n    94→\treturn &File{\n    95→\t\tbucket:       f.bucket,\n    96→\t\tkey:          name,\n    97→\t\tlength:       output.ContentLength,\n    98→\t\tbody:         output.Body,\n    99→\t\tlastModified: *output.LastModified,\n   100→\t}, nil\n   101→}\n   102→\n   103→// Stat implements fs.StatFS. For the s3 filesystem, this gets the\n   104→// objects in the s3 bucket and stores them for later use. Stat can\n   105→// only be called on the currect directory as the s3 filesystem only\n   106→// supports walking a single bucket configured at creation time.\n   107→func (f *FS) Stat(name string) (fs.FileInfo, error) {\n   108→\t// Stat can only be called on the current directory\n   109→\tif name != \".\" {\n   110→\t\treturn nil, &fs.PathError{\n   111→\t\t\tOp:   \"Stat\",\n   112→\t\t\tPath: name,\n   113→\t\t\tErr:  fs.ErrInvalid,\n   114→\t\t}\n   115→\t}\n   116→\tdirInfo := &FileInfo{\n   117→\t\tname: name,\n   118→\t\tsize: 0,\n   119→\t}\n   120→\tf.dirEntry = &Dir{\n   121→\t\tFileInfo: dirInfo,\n   122→\t}\n   123→\n   124→\t// AWS S3 does not store the last modified time for the bucket\n   125→\t// anywhere. We'd have to iterate through all the objects to\n   126→\t// calculate it, which doesn't seem worth it.\n   127→\n   128→\treturn f.dirEntry, nil\n   129→}\n   130→\n   131→// ReadDir implements fs.ReadDirFS. This can only be called on the\n   132→// current directory as the s3 filesystem does not support any kind of\n   133→// recursive directory structure\n   134→func (f *FS) ReadDir(name string) ([]fs.DirEntry, error) {\n   135→\t// ReadDir can only be called on the current directory, aka\n   136→\t// \".\" or the bucket\n   137→\tif name != \".\" && name != f.bucket {\n   138→\t\treturn nil, &fs.PathError{\n   139→\t\t\tOp:   \"ReadDir\",\n   140→\t\t\tPath: name,\n   141→\t\t\tErr:  fs.ErrInvalid,\n   142→\t\t}\n   143→\t}\n   144→\n   145→\t// If a prefix is provided, only list objects with that prefix\n   146→\t// This lets the user configure a portion of a bucket for\n   147→\t// feature flags, simulating a subdirectory.\n   148→\t//\n   149→\t// See https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/using-prefixes.html\n   150→\tvar prefix *string\n   151→\tif f.prefix != \"\" {\n   152→\t\tprefix = &f.prefix\n   153→\t}\n   154→\n   155→\t// instead of caching the entries in Open, fetch them here so\n   156→\t// if the list is large, they are not stored on the FS object.\n   157→\tentries := []fs.DirEntry{}\n   158→\n   159→\t// loop until all results are retrieved, but don't loop more\n   160→\t// than 100 times (creating 100,000 entries) as a safety\n   161→\t// measure to ensure we don't run out of memory and/or loop\n   162→\t// forever\n   163→\tvar continuationToken *string\n   164→\tfor i := 0; i < 100; i++ {\n   165→\t\toutput, err := f.s3Client.ListObjectsV2(context.Background(),\n   166→\t\t\t&s3.ListObjectsV2Input{\n   167→\t\t\t\tBucket:            &f.bucket,\n   168→\t\t\t\tPrefix:            prefix,\n   169→\t\t\t\tContinuationToken: continuationToken,\n   170→\t\t\t})\n   171→\t\tif err != nil {\n   172→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   173→\t\t}\n   174→\n   175→\t\tfor i := range output.Contents {\n   176→\t\t\tc := output.Contents[i]\n   177→\t\t\tfi := &FileInfo{\n   178→\t\t\t\tname:    *c.Key,\n   179→\t\t\t\tsize:    c.Size,\n   180→\t\t\t\tmodTime: *c.LastModified,\n   181→\t\t\t}\n   182→\t\t\tentries = append(entries, fi)\n   183→\t\t}\n   184→\t\tif !output.IsTruncated {\n   185→\t\t\treturn entries, nil\n   186→\t\t}\n   187→\t\tcontinuationToken = output.NextContinuationToken\n   188→\t}\n   189→\n   190→\t// We looped more than 100 times. 112→// Close cancels the polling routine and waits for the routine to return.\n   113→func (l *Store) Close() error {\n   114→\tl.cancel()\n   115→\n   116→\t<-l.done\n   117→\n   118→\treturn nil\n 74→\t\t\t\tname: \".\",\n    75→\t\t\t\tmode: fs.ModeDir,\n    76→\t\t\t\tmod:  created,\n    77→\t\t\t},\n    78→\t\t\tentries: entries,\n    79→\t\t}, nil\n    80→\tcase \".flipt.yml\":\n    81→\t\tindex := storagefs.FliptIndex{\n    82→\t\t\tVersion: \"1.0\",\n    83→\t\t}\n    84→\n    85→\t\tfor layer := range f.layers {\n    86→\t\t\tindex.Include = append(index.Include, layer)\n    87→\t\t}\n    88→\n    89→\t\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n    90→\t\tif err := yaml.NewEncoder(buf).Encode(&index); err != nil {\n    91→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n    92→\t\t}\n    93→\n    94→\t\treturn &File{\n    95→\t\t\tReadCloser: io.NopCloser(buf),\n    96→\t\t\tinfo: FileInfo{\n    97→\t\t\t\tname: \".flipt.yml\",\n    98→\t\t\t\tmod:  created,\n    99→\t\t\t\tmode: fs.ModePerm,\n   100→\t\t\t\tsize: int64(buf.Len()),\n   101→\t\t\t},\n   102→\t\t}, nil\n   103→\t}\n   104→\n   105→\tlayer, ok := f.layers[name]\n   106→\tif !ok {\n   107→\t\treturn nil, fs.ErrNotExist\n   108→\t}\n   109→\n   110→\trc, err := f.fetcher.Fetch(context.Background(), layer)\n   111→\tif err != nil {\n   112→\t\treturn nil, err\n   113→\t}\n   114→\n   115→\treturn &File{\n   116→\t\tReadCloser: rc,\n   117→\t\tinfo: FileInfo{\n   118→\t\t\tname: name,\n   119→\t\t\tmod:  created,\n   120→\t\t\tmode: fs.ModePerm,\n   121→\t\t\tsize: layer.Size,\n   122→\t\t},\n   123→\t}, nil\n   124→}\n   125→\n   126→type File struct {\n   127→\tio.ReadCloser\n   128→\n   129→\tinfo FileInfo\n   130→}\n   131→\n   132→// Seek attempts to seek the embedded read-closer.\n   133→// If the embedded read closer implements seek, then it delegates\n   134→// to that instances implementation. Alternatively, it returns\n   135→// an error signifying that the File cannot be seeked.\n   136→func (f *File) Seek(offset int64, whence int) (int64, error) {\n   137→\tif seek, ok := f.ReadCloser.(io.Seeker); ok {\n   138→\t\treturn seek.Seek(offset, whence)\n   139→\t}\n   140→\n   141→\treturn 0, errors.New(\"seeker cannot seek\")\n   142→}\n   143→\n   144→func (f *File) Stat() (fs.FileInfo, error) {\n   145→\treturn &f.info, nil\n   146→}\n   147→\n   148→type Dir struct {\n   149→\tinfo    FileInfo\n   150→\tentries []fs.DirEntry\n   151→\tidx     int\n   152→}\n   153→\n   154→func (d *Dir) Stat() (fs.FileInfo, error) {\n   155→\treturn d.info, nil\n   156→}\n   157→\n   158→func (d *Dir) Read(_ []byte) (int, error) {\n   159→\treturn 0, io.EOF\n   160→}\n   161→\n   162→func (d *Dir) Close() error {\n   163→\treturn nil\n   164→}\n   165→\n   166→// ReadDir reads the contents of the directory and returns\n   167→// a slice of up to n DirEntry values in directory order.\n   168→// Subsequent calls on the same file will yield further DirEntry values.\n   169→//\n   170→// If n > 0, ReadDir returns at most n DirEntry structures.\n   171→// In this case, if ReadDir returns an empty slice, it will return\n   172→// a non-nil error explaining why.\n   173→// At the end of a directory, the error is io.EOF.\n   174→// (ReadDir must return io.EOF itself, not an error wrapping io.EOF.)\n   175→//\n   176→// If n <= 0, ReadDir returns all the DirEntry values from the directory\n   177→// in a single slice. In this case, if ReadDir succeeds (reads all the way\n   178→// to the end of the directory), it returns the slice and a nil error.\n   179→// If it encounters an error before the end of the directory,\n   180→// ReadDir returns the DirEntry list read until that point and a non-nil error.\n   181→func (d *Dir) ReadDir(n int) (dst []fs.DirEntry, err error) {\n   182→\tif n <= 0 {\n   183→\t\treturn d.entries, nil\n   184→\t}\n   185→\n   186→\tl := len(d.entries[d.idx:])\n   187→\tif l <= 0 {\n   188→\t\treturn nil, io.EOF\n   189→\t}\n   190→\n   191→\tif l >= n {\n   192→\t\tl = n\n   193→\t}\n   194→\n   195→\tstart := d.idx\n   196→\n   197→\td.idx += l\n   198→\n   199→\treturn d.entries[start:d.idx], nil\n   200→}\n   201→\n   202→type FileInfo struct {\n   203→\tname string\n   204→\tsize int64\n   205→\tmode fs.FileMode\n   206→\tmod  time.Time\n   207→}\n   208→\n   209→func (f FileInfo) Name() string {\n   210→\treturn f.name\n   211→}\n   212→\n   213→func (f FileInfo) Size() int64 {\n   214→\treturn f.size\n   215→}\n   216→\n   217→func (f FileInfo) Mode() fs.FileMode {\n   218→\treturn f.mode\n   219→}\n   220→\n   221→func (f FileInfo) ModTime() time.Time {\n   222→\treturn f.mod\n   223→}\n   224→\n   225→func (f FileInfo) IsDir() bool {\n   226→\treturn f.mode.IsDir()\n   227→}\n   228→\n   229→func (f FileInfo) Sys() any {\n   230→\treturn nil\n   231→}\n   232→\n   233→type DirEntry struct {\n   234→\tdesc v1.Descriptor\n   235→\tmod  time.Time\n   236→}\n   237→\n   238→// Name returns the name of the file (or subdirectory) described by the entry.\n   239→// This name is only the final element of the path (the base name), not the entire path.\n   240→// For example, Name would return \"hello.go\" not \"home/gopher/hello.go\".\n   241→func (d *DirEntry) Name() string {\n   242→\treturn d.desc.Digest.Hex() + \".json\"\n   243→}\n   244→\n   245→// IsDir reports whether the entry describes a directory.\n   246→func (d *DirEntry) with a teardown function (Shutdown(ctx)).\n    95→type GRPCServer struct {\n    96→\t*grpc.Server\n    97→\n    98→\tlogger *zap.Logger\n    99→\tcfg    *config.Config\n   100→\tln     net.Listener\n   101→\n   102→\tshutdownFuncs []func(context.Context) error\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→// NewGRPCServer constructs the core Flipt gRPC service including its dependencies\n   106→// (e.g. tracing, metrics, storage, migrations, caching and cleanup).\n   107→// It returns an instance of *GRPCServer which callers can Run().\n   108→func NewGRPCServer(\n   109→\tctx context.Context,\n   110→\tlogger *zap.Logger,\n   111→\tcfg *config.Config,\n   112→\tinfo info.Flipt,\n   113→\tforceMigrate bool,\n   114→) (*GRPCServer, error) {\n   115→\tlogger = logger.With(zap.String(\"server\", \"grpc\"))\n   116→\tserver := &GRPCServer{\n   117→\t\tlogger: logger,\n   118→\t\tcfg:    cfg,\n   119→\t}\n   120→\n   121→\tvar err error\n   122→\tserver.ln, err = net.Listen(\"tcp\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d\", cfg.Server.Host, cfg.Server.GRPCPort))\n   123→\tif err != nil {\n   124→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"creating grpc listener: %w\", err)\n   125→\t}\n   126→\n   127→\tserver.onShutdown(func(context.Context) error {\n   128→\t\treturn server.ln.Close()\n   129→\t})\n   130→\n   131→\tvar store storage.Store\n   132→\n   133→\tswitch cfg.Storage.Type {\n   134→\tcase \"\", config.DatabaseStorageType:\n   135→\t\tdb, builder, driver, dbShutdown, err := getDB(ctx, logger, cfg, forceMigrate)\n   136→\t\tif err != nil {\n   137→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   138→\t\t}\n   139→\n   140→\t\tserver.onShutdown(dbShutdown)\n   141→\n   142→\t\tswitch driver {\n   143→\t\tcase fliptsql.SQLite, fliptsql.LibSQL:\n   144→\t\t\tstore = sqlite.NewStore(db, builder, logger)\n   145→\t\tcase fliptsql.Postgres, fliptsql.CockroachDB:\n   146→\t\t\tstore = postgres.NewStore(db, builder, logger)\n   147→\t\tcase fliptsql.MySQL:\n   148→\t\t\tstore = mysql.NewStore(db, builder, logger)\n   149→\t\tdefault:\n   150→\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported driver: %s\", driver)\n   151→\t\t}\n   152→\n   153→\t\tlogger.Debug(\"database driver configured\", zap.Stringer(\"driver\", driver))\n   154→\tcase config.GitStorageType:\n   155→\t\topts := []containers.Option[git.Source]{\n   156→\t\t\tgit.WithRef(cfg.Storage.Git.Ref),\n   157→\t\t\tgit.WithPollInterval(cfg.Storage.Git.PollInterval),\n   158→\t\t}\n   159→\n   160→\t\tauth := cfg.Storage.Git.Authentication\n   161→\t\tswitch {\n   162→\t\tcase auth.BasicAuth != nil:\n   163→\t\t\topts = append(opts, git.WithAuth(&http.BasicAuth{\n   164→\t\t\t\tUsername: auth.BasicAuth.Username,\n   165→\t\t\t\tPassword: auth.BasicAuth.Password,\n   166→\t\t\t}))\n   167→\t\tcase auth.TokenAuth != nil:\n   168→\t\t\topts = append(opts, git.WithAuth(&http.TokenAuth{\n   169→\t\t\t\tToken: auth.TokenAuth.AccessToken,\n   170→\t\t\t}))\n   171→\t\tcase auth.SSHAuth != nil:\n   172→\t\t\tvar method *gitssh.PublicKeys\n   173→\t\t\tif auth.SSHAuth.PrivateKeyBytes != \"\" {\n   174→\t\t\t\tmethod, err = gitssh.NewPublicKeys(\n   175→\t\t\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.User,\n   176→\t\t\t\t\t[]byte(auth.SSHAuth.PrivateKeyBytes),\n   177→\t\t\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.Password,\n   178→\t\t\t\t)\n   179→\t\t\t} else {\n   180→\t\t\t\tmethod, err = gitssh.NewPublicKeysFromFile(\n   181→\t\t\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.User,\n   182→\t\t\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.PrivateKeyPath,\n   183→\t\t\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.Password,\n   184→\t\t\t\t)\n   185→\t\t\t}\n   186→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   187→\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   188→\t\t\t}\n   189→\n   190→\t\t\t// we're protecting against this explicitly so we can disable\n   191→\t\t\t// the gosec linting rule\n   192→\t\t\tif auth.SSHAuth.InsecureIgnoreHostKey {\n   193→\t\t\t\t// nolint:gosec\n   194→\t\t\t\tmethod.HostKeyCallback = ssh.InsecureIgnoreHostKey()\n   195→\t\t\t}\n   196→\n   197→\t\t\topts = append(opts, git.WithAuth(method))\n   198→\t\t}\n   199→\n   200→\t\tsource, err := git.NewSource(logger, cfg.Storage.Git.Repository, opts...)\n   201→\t\tif err != nil {\n   202→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   203→\t\t}\n   204→\n   205→\t\tstore, err = fs.NewStore(logger, source)\n   206→\t\tif err != nil {\n   207→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   208→\t\t}\n   209→\tcase config.LocalStorageType:\n   210→\t\tsource, err := local.NewSource(logger, cfg.Storage.Local.Path)\n   211→\t\tif err != nil {\n   212→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   213→\t\t}\n   214→\n   215→\t\tstore, err = fs.NewStore(logger, source)\n   216→\t\tif err != nil {\n   217→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   218→\t\t}\n   219→\tcase config.ObjectStorageType:\n   220→\t\tstore, err = NewObjectStore(cfg, logger)\n   221→\t\tif err != nil {\n   222→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   223→\t\t}\n   224→\tcase config.OCIStorageType:\n   225→\t\tsource, err := oci.NewSource(logger, cfg.Storage.OCI.Repository)\n   226→\t\tif err != nil {\n   227→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   228→\t\t}\n   229→\n   230→\t\tstore, err = fs.NewStore(logger, source)\n   231→\t\tif err != nil {\n   232→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   233→\t\t}\n   234→\tdefault:\n   235→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"unexpected storage type: %q\", cfg.Storage.Type)\n   236→\t}\n   237→\n   238→\tlogger.Debug(\"store enabled\", zap.Stringer(\"store\", store))\n   239→\n   240→\t// Initialize tracingProvider regardless of configuration. No extraordinary resources\n   241→\t// are consumed, or goroutines initialized until a SpanProcessor is registered.\n   242→\tvar tracingProvider = tracesdk.NewTracerProvider(\n   243→\t\ttracesdk.WithResource(resource.NewWithAttributes(\n   244→\t\t\tsemconv.SchemaURL,\n   245→\t\t\tsemconv.ServiceNameKey.String(\"flipt\"),\n   246→\t\t\tsemconv.ServiceVersionKey.String(info.Version),\n   247→\t\t)),\n   248→\t\ttracesdk.WithSampler(tracesdk.AlwaysSample()),\n   249→\t)\n   250→\n   251→\tif cfg.Tracing.Enabled {\n   252→\t\texp, traceExpShutdown, err := getTraceExporter(ctx, cfg)\n   253→\t\tif err != nil {\n   254→\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"creating tracing exporter: %w\", err)\n   255→\t\t}\n   256→\n   257→\t\tserver.onShutdown(traceExpShutdown)\n   258→\n   259→\t\ttracingProvider.RegisterSpanProcessor(tracesdk.NewBatchSpanProcessor(exp, tracesdk.WithBatchTimeout(1*time.Second)))\n   260→\n   261→\t\tlogger.Debug(\"otel tracing enabled\", zap.String(\"exporter\", cfg.Tracing.Exporter.String()))\n   262→\t}\n   263→\n   264→\t// base observability inteceptors\n   265→\tinterceptors := []grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor{\n   266→\t\tgrpc_recovery.UnaryServerInterceptor(grpc_recovery.WithRecoveryHandler(func(p interface{}) (err error) {\n   267→\t\t\tlogger.Error(\"panic recovered\", zap.Any(\"panic\", p))\n   268→\t\t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.Internal, \"%v\", p)\n   269→\t\t})),\n   270→\t\tgrpc_ctxtags.UnaryServerInterceptor(),\n   271→\t\tgrpc_zap.UnaryServerInterceptor(logger),\n   272→\t\tgrpc_prometheus.UnaryServerInterceptor,\n   273→\t\totelgrpc.UnaryServerInterceptor(),\n   274→\t}\n   275→\n   276→\tvar cacher cache.Cacher\n   277→\tif cfg.Cache.Enabled {\n   278→\t\tvar (\n   279→\t\t\tcacheShutdown errFunc\n   280→\t\t\terr           error\n   281→\t\t)\n   282→\t\tcacher, cacheShutdown, err = getCache(ctx, cfg)\n   283→\n   284→\t\tif err != nil {\n   285→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   286→\t\t}\n   287→\n   288→\t\tserver.onShutdown(cacheShutdown)\n   289→\n   290→\t\tstore = storagecache.NewStore(store, cacher, logger)\n   291→\n   292→\t\tlogger.Debug(\"cache enabled\", zap.Stringer(\"backend\", cacher))\n   293→\t}\n   294→\n   295→\tvar (\n   296→\t\tfliptsrv           = fliptserver.New(logger, store)\n   297→\t\tmetasrv            = metadata.New(cfg, info)\n   298→\t\tevalsrv            = evaluation.New(logger, store)\n   299→\t\tauthOpts           = []containers.Option[auth.InterceptorOptions]{}\n   300→\t\tskipAuthIfExcluded = func(server any, excluded bool) {\n   301→\t\t\tif excluded {\n   302→\t\t\t\tauthOpts = append(authOpts, auth.WithServerSkipsAuthentication(server))\n   303→\t\t\t}\n   304→\t\t}\n   305→\t)\n   306→\n   307→\tskipAuthIfExcluded(fliptsrv, cfg.Authentication.Exclude.Management)\n   308→\tskipAuthIfExcluded(metasrv, cfg.Authentication.Exclude.Metadata)\n   309→\tskipAuthIfExcluded(evalsrv, cfg.Authentication.Exclude.Evaluation)\n   310→\n   311→\tvar checker *audit.Checker\n   312→\n   313→\t// We have to check if audit logging is enabled here for informing the authentication service that\n   314→\t// the user would like to receive token:deleted events.\n   315→\tif 322→\n   323→\tvar tokenDeletedEnabled bool\n   324→\tif checker != nil {\n   325→\t\ttokenDeletedEnabled = checker.Check(\"token:deleted\")\n   326→\t}\n   327→\n   328→\tregister, authInterceptors, authShutdown, err := authenticationGRPC(\n   329→\t\tctx,\n   330→\t\tlogger,\n   331→\t\tcfg,\n   332→\t\tforceMigrate,\n   333→\t\ttokenDeletedEnabled,\n   334→\t\tauthOpts...,\n   335→\t)\n   336→\tif err != nil {\n   337→\t\treturn nil, err\n   338→\t}\n   339→\n   340→\tserver.onShutdown(authShutdown)\n   341→\n   342→\t// initialize servers\n   343→\tregister.Add(fliptsrv)\n   344→\tregister.Add(metasrv)\n   345→\tregister.Add(evalsrv)\n   346→\n   347→\t// forward internal gRPC logging to zap\n   348→\tgrpcLogLevel, err := zapcore.ParseLevel(cfg.Log.GRPCLevel)\n   349→\tif err != nil {\n   350→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"parsing grpc log level (%q): %w\", cfg.Log.GRPCLevel, err)\n   351→\t}\n   352→\n   353→\tgrpc_zap.ReplaceGrpcLoggerV2(logger.WithOptions(zap.IncreaseLevel(grpcLogLevel)))\n   354→\n   355→\t// add auth interceptors to the server\n   356→\tinterceptors = append(interceptors,\n   357→\t\tappend(authInterceptors,\n   358→\t\t\tmiddlewaregrpc.ErrorUnaryInterceptor,\n   359→\t\t\tmiddlewaregrpc.ValidationUnaryInterceptor,\n   360→\t\t\tmiddlewaregrpc.EvaluationUnaryInterceptor,\n   361→\t\t)...,\n   362→\t)\n   363→\n   364→\t// cache must come after auth interceptors\n   365→\tif cfg.Cache.Enabled && cacher != nil {\n   366→\t\tinterceptors = append(interceptors, middlewaregrpc.CacheUnaryInterceptor(cacher, logger))\n   367→\t}\n   368→\n   369→\t// audit sinks configuration\n   370→\tsinks := make([]audit.Sink, 0)\n   371→\n   372→\tif cfg.Audit.Sinks.LogFile.Enabled {\n   373→\t\tlogFileSink, err := logfile.NewSink(logger, cfg.Audit.Sinks.LogFile.File)\n   374→\t\tif err != nil {\n   375→\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"opening file at path: %s\", cfg.Audit.Sinks.LogFile.File)\n   376→\t\t}\n   377→\n   378→\t\tsinks = append(sinks, logFileSink)\n   379→\t}\n   380→\n   381→\tif cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.Enabled {\n   382→\t\topts := []webhook.ClientOption{}\n   383→\t\tif cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.MaxBackoffDuration > 0 {\n   384→\t\t\topts = append(opts, webhook.WithMaxBackoffDuration(cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.MaxBackoffDuration))\n   385→\t\t}\n   386→\n   387→\t\tvar webhookSink audit.Sink\n   388→\n   389→\t\t// Enable basic webhook sink if URL is non-empty, otherwise enable template sink if the length of templates is greater\n   390→\t\t// than 0 for the webhook.\n   391→\t\tif cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.URL != \"\" {\n   392→\t\t\twebhookSink = webhook.NewSink(logger, webhook.NewWebhookClient(logger, cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.URL, cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.SigningSecret, opts...))\n   393→\t\t} else if len(cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.Templates) > 0 {\n   394→\t\t\tmaxBackoffDuration := 15 * time.Second\n   395→\t\t\tif cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.MaxBackoffDuration > 0 {\n   396→\t\t\t\tmaxBackoffDuration = cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.MaxBackoffDuration\n   397→\t\t\t}\n   398→\n   399→\t\t\twebhookSink, err = template.NewSink(logger, cfg.Audit.Sinks.Webhook.Templates, maxBackoffDuration)\n   400→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   401→\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   402→\t\t\t}\n   403→\t\t}\n   404→\n   405→\t\tsinks = append(sinks, webhookSink)\n   406→\t}\n   407→\n   408→\t// based on audit sink configuration from the user, provision the audit sinks and add them to a slice,\n   409→\t// and if the slice has a non-zero length, add the audit sink interceptor\n   410→\tif len(sinks) > 0 {\n   411→\t\tsse := audit.NewSinkSpanExporter(logger, sinks)\n   412→\t\ttracingProvider.RegisterSpanProcessor(tracesdk.NewBatchSpanProcessor(sse, tracesdk.WithBatchTimeout(cfg.Audit.Buffer.FlushPeriod), tracesdk.WithMaxExportBatchSize(cfg.Audit.Buffer.Capacity)))\n   413→\n   414→\t\tinterceptors = append(interceptors, middlewaregrpc.AuditUnaryInterceptor(logger, checker))\n   415→\t\tlogger.Debug(\"audit sinks enabled\",\n   416→\t\t\tzap.Stringers(\"sinks\", sinks),\n   417→\t\t\tzap.Int(\"buffer capacity\", cfg.Audit.Buffer.Capacity),\n   418→\t\t\tzap.String(\"flush period\", cfg.Audit.Buffer.FlushPeriod.String()),\n   419→\t\t\tzap.Strings(\"events\", checker.Events()),\n   420→\t\t)\n   421→\n   422→\t\tserver.onShutdown(func(ctx context.Context) error {\n   423→\t\t\treturn sse.Shutdown(ctx)\n   424→\t\t})\n   425→\t}\n   426→\n   427→\tserver.onShutdown(func(ctx context.Context) error {\n   428→\t\treturn tracingProvider.Shutdown(ctx)\n   429→\t})\n   430→\n   431→\totel.SetTracerProvider(tracingProvider)\n   432→\totel.SetTextMapPropagator(propagation.NewCompositeTextMapPropagator(propagation.TraceContext{}, propagation.Baggage{}))\n   433→\n   434→\tgrpcOpts := []grpc.ServerOption{grpc.ChainUnaryInterceptor(interceptors...)}\n   435→\n   436→\tif cfg.Server.Protocol == config.HTTPS {\n   437→\t\tcreds, err := credentials.NewServerTLSFromFile(cfg.Server.CertFile, cfg.Server.CertKey)\n   438→\t\tif err != nil {\n   439→\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"loading TLS credentials: %w\", err)\n   440→\t\t}\n   441→\n   442→\t\tgrpcOpts = append(grpcOpts, grpc.Creds(creds))\n   443→\t}\n   444→\n   445→\t// initialize grpc server\n   446→\tserver.Server = grpc.NewServer(grpcOpts...)\n   447→\n   448→\thealthserver := health.NewServer()\n   449→\tgrpc_health.RegisterHealthServer(server.Server, healthserver)\n   450→\n   451→\t// register grpcServer graceful stop on shutdown\n   452→\tserver.onShutdown(func(context.Context) error {\n   453→\t\thealthserver.Shutdown()\n   454→\t\tserver.GracefulStop()\n   455→\t\treturn nil\n   456→\t})\n   457→\n   458→\t// register each grpc service onto the grpc server\n   459→\tregister.RegisterGRPC(server.Server)\n   460→\n   461→\tgrpc_prometheus.EnableHandlingTimeHistogram()\n   462→\tgrpc_prometheus.Register(server.Server)\n   463→\treflection.Register(server.Server)\n   464→\n   465→\treturn server, nil\n   466→}\n   467→\n   468→// NewObjectStore create a new storate.Store from the object config\n   469→func NewObjectStore(cfg *config.Config, logger *zap.Logger) (storage.Store, error) {\n   470→\tobjectCfg := cfg.Storage.Object\n   471→\tvar store storage.Store\n   472→\t// keep this as a case statement in anticipation of\n   473→\t// more object types in the future\n   474→\t// nolint:gocritic\n   475→\tswitch objectCfg.Type {\n   476→\tcase config.S3ObjectSubStorageType:\n   477→\t\topts := []containers.Option[s3.Source]{\n   478→\t\t\ts3.WithPollInterval(objectCfg.S3.PollInterval),\n   479→\t\t}\n   480→\t\tif objectCfg.S3.Endpoint != \"\" {\n   481→\t\t\topts = append(opts, s3.WithEndpoint(objectCfg.S3.Endpoint))\n   482→\t\t}\n   483→\t\tif objectCfg.S3.Region != \"\" {\n   484→\t\t\topts = append(opts, s3.WithRegion(objectCfg.S3.Region))\n   485→\t\t}\n   486→\t\tsource, err := s3.NewSource(logger, objectCfg.S3.Bucket, opts...)\n   487→\t\tif err != nil {\n   488→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   489→\t\t}\n   490→\t\tstore, err = fs.NewStore(logger, source)\n   491→\t\tif err != nil {\n   492→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   493→\t\t}\n   494→\t}\n   495→\treturn store, nil\n   496→}\n   497→\n   498→// Run begins serving gRPC requests.\n   499→// This methods blocks until Shutdown is called.\n   500→func (s *GRPCServer) Run() error {\n   501→\ts.logger.Debug(\"starting grpc server\")\n   502→\n   503→\treturn s.Serve(s.ln)\n   504→}\n   505→\n   506→// Shutdown tearsdown the entire gRPC stack including dependencies.\n   507→func (s *GRPCServer) Shutdown(ctx context.Context) error {\n   508→\ts.logger.Info(\"shutting down GRPC server...\")\n   509→\n   510→\t// call in reverse order to emulate pop semantics of a stack\n   511→\tfor i := len(s.shutdownFuncs) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {\n   512→\t\tif fn := s.shutdownFuncs[i]; fn != nil {\n   513→\t\t\tif err := fn(ctx); err != nil {\n   514→\t\t\t\treturn err\n   515→\t\t\t}\n   516→\t\t}\n   517→\t}\n   518→\n   519→\treturn nil\n   520→}\n   521→\n   522→type errFunc func(context.Context) error\n   523→\n   524→func (s *GRPCServer) onShutdown(fn errFunc) {\n   525→\ts.shutdownFuncs = append(s.shutdownFuncs, fn)\n   526→}\n   527→\n   528→var (\n   529→\ttraceExpOnce sync.Once\n   530→\ttraceExp     tracesdk.SpanExporter\n   531→\ttraceExpFunc errFunc = func(context.Context) error { return nil }\n   532→\ttraceExpErr  error\n   533→)\n   534→\n   535→func getTraceExporter(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config) (tracesdk.SpanExporter, errFunc, error) {\n   536→\ttraceExpOnce.Do(func() {\n   537→\t\tswitch cfg.Tracing.Exporter {\n   538→\t\tcase config.TracingJaeger:\n   539→\t\t\ttraceExp, traceExpErr = jaeger.New(jaeger.WithAgentEndpoint(\n   540→\t\t\t\tjaeger.WithAgentHost(cfg.Tracing.Jaeger.Host),\n   541→\t\t\t\tjaeger.WithAgentPort(strconv.FormatInt(int64(cfg.Tracing.Jaeger.Port), 10)),\n   542→\t\t\t))\n   543→\t\tcase config.TracingZipkin:\n   544→\t\t\ttraceExp, traceExpErr = zipkin.New(cfg.Tracing.Zipkin.Endpoint)\n   545→\t\tcase config.TracingOTLP:\n   546→\t\t\tu, err := url.Parse(cfg.Tracing.OTLP.Endpoint)\n   547→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   548→\t\t\t\ttraceExpErr = fmt.Errorf(\"parsing otlp endpoint: %w\", err)\n   549→\t\t\t\treturn\n   550→\t\t\t}\n   551→\n   552→\t\t\tvar client otlptrace.Client\n   553→\t\t\tswitch u.Scheme {\n   554→\t\t\tcase \"http\", \"https\":\n   555→\t\t\t\tclient = otlptracehttp.NewClient(\n   556→\t\t\t\t\totlptracehttp.WithEndpoint(u.Host+u.Path),\n   557→\t\t\t\t\totlptracehttp.WithHeaders(cfg.Tracing.OTLP.Headers),\n   558→\t\t\t\t)\n   559→\t\t\tcase \"grpc\":\n   560→\t\t\t\t// TODO: support additional configuration options\n   561→\t\t\t\tclient = otlptracegrpc.NewClient(\n   562→\t\t\t\t\totlptracegrpc.WithEndpoint(u.Host+u.Path),\n   563→\t\t\t\t\totlptracegrpc.WithHeaders(cfg.Tracing.OTLP.Headers),\n   564→\t\t\t\t\t// TODO: support TLS\n   565→\t\t\t\t\totlptracegrpc.WithInsecure(),\n   566→\t\t\t\t)\n   567→\t\t\tdefault:\n   568→\t\t\t\t// because of url parsing ambiguity, we'll assume that the endpoint is a host:port with no scheme\n   569→\t\t\t\tclient = otlptracegrpc.NewClient(\n   570→\t\t\t\t\totlptracegrpc.WithEndpoint(cfg.Tracing.OTLP.Endpoint),\n   571→\t\t\t\t\totlptracegrpc.WithHeaders(cfg.Tracing.OTLP.Headers),\n The most straightforward method using username/password pairs\n2. **Credential Cache** - Built-in caching to avoid repeated authentication\n3. **Custom Credential Providers** - Extensible system for implementing custom authentication logic\n\n## How to Authenticate a Remote Repository\n\nHere's the standard pattern from the documentation examples:\n\n```go\nimport (\n    \"oras.land/oras-go/v2/registry/remote\"\n    \"oras.land/oras-go/v2/registry/remote/auth\"\n    \"oras.land/oras-go/v2/registry/remote/retry\"\n)\n\n// Connect to a remote repository\nreg := \"myregistry.example.com\"\nrepo, err := remote.NewRepository(reg + \"/myrepo\")\nif err != nil {\n    panic(err)\n}\n\n// Configure authentication\nrepo.Client = &auth.Client{\n    Client: retry.DefaultClient,\n    Cache:  auth.DefaultCache,\n    Credential: auth.StaticCredential(reg, auth.Credential{\n        Username: \"username\",\n        Password: \"password\",\n    }),\n}\n```\n\n## Key Components\n\n- **`auth.Client`** - Wraps HTTP client with authentication support\n- **`retry.DefaultClient`** - Handles retries with exponential backoff\n- **`auth.DefaultCache`** - Caches credentials to improve performance\n- **`auth.StaticCredential()`** - Creates static username/password credentials\n- **`auth.Credential`** - Struct containing Username and Password fields\n\nThe authentication is applied at the HTTP client level, so all subsequent registry operations through that repository will use the configured credentials."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_014i2dCMAskAjKxUzbQAPHbQ","session_id":"9242c96e-345e-487b-a52c-90157cb0ee2d","uuid":"6e515ad7-8495-4e67-ad1a-d55e54f2e461"}
