{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014vN5ckzY4ZRSfHAgrWmTcv","type":"tool_result","content":"# Credential Types and Authentication Methods in the Auth Package\n\nBased on the documentation, the `auth` package provides the following credential types and authentication methods:\n\n## Credential Types\n\nThe `Credential` struct supports multiple authentication approaches:\n\n```go\ntype Credential struct {\n    Username string      // Username for the remote registry\n    Password string      // Password associated with username\n    RefreshToken string  // Bearer token for fetching access tokens (OAuth2)\n    AccessToken string   // Bearer token to send directly to registry\n}\n```\n\n## Authentication Schemes\n\nThe package defines three authentication schemes:\n\n1. **SchemeBasic** - HTTP Basic authentication (RFC 7617)\n2. **SchemeBearer** - Bearer token authentication (OAuth 2.0, RFC 6750)\n3. **SchemeUnknown** - Unknown or unsupported schemes\n\n## Supported Authentication Methods\n\nThe `Client` type supports the following authentication patterns:\n\n### 1. **Basic Authentication** (Username/Password)\n```go\nclient := &auth.Client{\n    Credential: auth.StaticCredential(expectedHostAddress, auth.Credential{\n        Username: username,\n        Password: password,\n    }),\n}\n```\n\n### 2. **Access Token**\n```go\nclient := &auth.Client{\n    Credential: auth.StaticCredential(expectedHostAddress, auth.Credential{\n        AccessToken: accessToken,\n    }),\n}\n```\n\n### 3. **Refresh Token** (OAuth2)\n```go\nclient := &auth.Client{\n    Credential: auth.StaticCredential(expectedHostAddress, auth.Credential{\n        RefreshToken: refreshToken,\n    }),\n}\n```\n\n### 4. **OAuth2 Password Grant**\nThe `Client` has a `ForceAttemptOAuth2` field to enforce OAuth2 authentication instead of the distribution spec when using username and password.\n\n## Key Features\n\n- **Caching**: Built-in `Cache` interface for storing authentication tokens\n- **Scopes**: Support for Docker registry authentication scopes (pull, push, delete)\n- **User Agent**: Customizable user agent via `SetUserAgent()`\n- **Static Credentials**: `StaticCredential()` helper for specifying credentials per registry"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_014i2dCMAskAjKxUzbQAPHbQ","session_id":"9242c96e-345e-487b-a52c-90157cb0ee2d","uuid":"c350c94f-17d6-40dd-ab6c-38e0b3f052e6"}

OCI Struct and Configuration (internal/config/storage.go)\n\nThe OCI struct is quite minimal:\n```go\ntype OCI struct {\n\tRepository string `json:\"repository,omitempty\" mapstructure:\"repository\" yaml:\"repository,omitempty\"`\n}\n```\n\n**Key observation**: The OCI config currently only has a Repository field - there is NO authentication configuration structure defined, unlike Git storage.\n\n### 2. Git Storage Authentication Pattern (internal/config/storage.go)\n\nGit storage implements a sophisticated multi-auth pattern:\n\n```go\ntype Authentication struct {\n\tBasicAuth *BasicAuth `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"basic,omitempty\" yaml:\"-\"`\n\tTokenAuth *TokenAuth `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"token,omitempty\" yaml:\"-\"`\n\tSSHAuth   *SSHAuth   `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"ssh,omitempty\" yaml:\"-\"`\n}\n\ntype BasicAuth struct {\n\tUsername string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"username\" yaml:\"-\"`\n\tPassword string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"password\" yaml:\"-\"`\n}\n\ntype TokenAuth struct {\n\tAccessToken string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"access_token\" yaml:\"-\"`\n}\n\ntype SSHAuth struct {\n\tUser                  string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"user\" yaml:\"-\" `\n\tPassword              string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"password\" yaml:\"-\" `\n\tPrivateKeyBytes       string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"private_key_bytes\" yaml:\"-\" `\n\tPrivateKeyPath        string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"private_key_path\" yaml:\"-\" `\n\tInsecureIgnoreHostKey bool   `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"insecure_ignore_host_key\" yaml:\"-\"`\n}\n```\n\n**Key patterns**:\n- Uses pointers to make auth types optional (`*BasicAuth`, `*TokenAuth`, `*SSHAuth`)\n- All fields are excluded from JSON/YAML marshaling (`json:\"-\" yaml:\"-\"`)\n- Uses mapstructure for configuration parsing\n\n### 3. Validation Pattern (internal/config/storage.go)\n\nThe Authentication struct implements a validation chain:\n\n```go\nfunc (a *Authentication) validate() error {\n\tif a.BasicAuth != nil {\n\t\tif err := a.BasicAuth.validate(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif a.TokenAuth != nil {\n\t\tif err := a.TokenAuth.validate(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif a.SSHAuth != nil {\n\t\tif err := a.SSHAuth.validate(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// BasicAuth validation - ensures both username AND password are provided\nfunc (b BasicAuth) validate() error {\n\tif (b.Username != \"\" && b.Password == \"\") || (b.Username == \"\" && b.Password != \"\") {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"both username and password need to be provided for basic auth\")\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// SSHAuth validation - ensures password and either PrivateKeyBytes OR PrivateKeyPath (but not both)\nfunc (a SSHAuth) validate() (err error) {\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terr = fmt.Errorf(\"ssh authentication: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\tif a.Password == \"\" {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"password required\")\n\t}\n\tif (a.PrivateKeyBytes == \"\" && a.PrivateKeyPath == \"\") || (a.PrivateKeyBytes != \"\" && a.PrivateKeyPath != \"\") {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"please provide exclusively one of private_key_bytes or private_key_path\")\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n```\n\n### 4. How Credentials Flow from Config to Storage Implementation (internal/cmd/grpc.go)\n\nThe pattern uses a switch statement to examine which auth type is populated and convert it to the appropriate go-git transport method:\n\n```go\nauth := cfg.Storage.Git.Authentication\nswitch {\ncase auth.BasicAuth != nil:\n\topts = append(opts, git.WithAuth(&http.BasicAuth{\n\t\tUsername: auth.BasicAuth.Username,\n\t\tPassword: auth.BasicAuth.Password,\n\t}))\ncase auth.TokenAuth != nil:\n\topts = append(opts, git.WithAuth(&http.TokenAuth{\n\t\tToken: auth.TokenAuth.AccessToken,\n\t}))\ncase auth.SSHAuth != nil:\n\tvar method *gitssh.PublicKeys\n\tif auth.SSHAuth.PrivateKeyBytes != \"\" {\n\t\tmethod, err = gitssh.NewPublicKeys(\n\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.User,\n\t\t\t[]byte(auth.SSHAuth.PrivateKeyBytes),\n\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.Password,\n\t\t)\n\t} else {\n\t\tmethod, err = gitssh.NewPublicKeysFromFile(\n\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.User,\n\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.PrivateKeyPath,\n\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.Password,\n\t\t)\n\t}\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tif auth.SSHAuth.InsecureIgnoreHostKey {\n\t\tmethod.HostKeyCallback = ssh.InsecureIgnoreHostKey()\n\t}\n\topts = append(opts, git.WithAuth(method))\n}\n```\n\n**Key patterns**:\n- Uses mutually exclusive switch with multiple auth types\n- Converts config types to go-git transport types\n- Uses options pattern (`git.WithAuth()`) to configure Source\n- Handles both static credentials and file-based credentials (for SSH keys)\n\n### 5. Git Storage Source Implementation (internal/storage/fs/git/source.go)\n\nThe Source struct stores the auth method as a `transport.AuthMethod` interface:\n\n```go\ntype Source struct {\n\tlogger *zap.Logger\n\trepo   *git.Repository\n\turl    string\n\tref    string\n\thash   plumbing.Hash\n\tinterval time.Duration\n\tauth   transport.AuthMethod  // <-- Accepts any auth method\n}\n\n// WithAuth returns an option which configures the auth method used by the provided source\nfunc WithAuth(auth transport.AuthMethod) containers.Option[Source] {\n\treturn func(s *Source) {\n\t\ts.auth = auth\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSource uses auth in both Clone and Fetch\nfunc (s *Source) NewSource(...) {\n\tsource.repo, err = git.Clone(memory.NewStorage(), nil, &git.CloneOptions{\n\t\tAuth: source.auth,  // <-- Passed to Clone\n\t\tURL:  source.url,\n\t})\n}\n\n// Subscribe also uses auth for periodic Fetch operations\nfunc (s *Source) Subscribe(ctx context.Context, ch chan<- fs.FS) {\n\t// ... in ticker loop:\n\tif err := s.repo.Fetch(&git.FetchOptions{\n\t\tAuth: s.auth,  // <-- Reused for Fetch\n\t\t// ...\n\t})\n}\n```\n\n### 6. Git Storage Authentication Pattern (internal/config/storage.go)\n\nGit storage implements a sophisticated multi-auth pattern:\n\n```go\ntype Authentication struct {\n\tBasicAuth *BasicAuth `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"basic,omitempty\" yaml:\"-\"`\n\tTokenAuth *TokenAuth `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"token,omitempty\" yaml:\"-\"`\n\tSSHAuth   *SSHAuth   `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"ssh,omitempty\" yaml:\"-\"`\n}\n\ntype BasicAuth struct {\n\tUsername string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"username\" yaml:\"-\"`\n\tPassword string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"password\" yaml:\"-\"`\n}\n\ntype TokenAuth struct {\n\tAccessToken string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"access_token\" yaml:\"-\"`\n}\n\ntype SSHAuth struct {\n\tUser                  string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"user\" yaml:\"-\" `\n\tPassword              string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"password\" yaml:\"-\" `\n\tPrivateKeyBytes       string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"private_key_bytes\" yaml:\"-\" `\n\tPrivateKeyPath        string `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"private_key_path\" yaml:\"-\" `\n\tInsecureIgnoreHostKey bool   `json:\"-\" mapstructure:\"insecure_ignore_host_key\" yaml:\"-\"`\n}\n```\n\n**Key patterns**:\n- Uses pointers to make auth types optional (`*BasicAuth`, `*TokenAuth`, `*SSHAuth`)\n- All fields are excluded from JSON/YAML marshaling (`json:\"-\" yaml:\"-\"`)\n- Uses mapstructure for configuration parsing\n\n### 3. Validation Pattern (internal/config/storage.go)\n\nThe Authentication struct implements a validation chain:\n\n```go\nfunc (a *Authentication) validate() error {\n\tif a.BasicAuth != nil {\n\t\tif err := a.BasicAuth.validate(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif a.TokenAuth != nil {\n\t\tif err := a.TokenAuth.validate(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif a.SSHAuth != nil {\n\t\tif err := a.SSHAuth.validate(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// BasicAuth validation - ensures both username AND password are provided\nfunc (b BasicAuth) validate() error {\n\tif (b.Username != \"\" && b.Password == \"\") || (b.Username == \"\" && b.Password != \"\") {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"both username and password need to be provided for basic auth\")\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// SSHAuth validation - ensures password and either PrivateKeyBytes OR PrivateKeyPath (but not both)\nfunc (a SSHAuth) validate() (err error) {\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terr = fmt.Errorf(\"ssh authentication: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\tif a.Password == \"\" {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"password required\")\n\t}\n\tif (a.PrivateKeyBytes == \"\" && a.PrivateKeyPath == \"\") || (a.PrivateKeyBytes != \"\" && a.PrivateKeyPath != \"\") {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"please provide exclusively one of private_key_bytes or private_key_path\")\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n```\n\n### 4. How Credentials Flow from Config to Storage Implementation (internal/cmd/grpc.go)\n\nThe pattern uses a switch statement to examine which auth type is populated and convert it to the appropriate go-git transport method:\n\n```go\nauth := cfg.Storage.Git.Authentication\nswitch {\ncase auth.BasicAuth != nil:\n\topts = append(opts, git.WithAuth(&http.BasicAuth{\n\t\tUsername: auth.BasicAuth.Username,\n\t\tPassword: auth.BasicAuth.Password,\n\t}))\ncase auth.TokenAuth != nil:\n\topts = append(opts, git.WithAuth(&http.TokenAuth{\n\t\tToken: auth.TokenAuth.AccessToken,\n\t}))\ncase auth.SSHAuth != nil:\n\tvar method *gitssh.PublicKeys\n\tif auth.SSHAuth.PrivateKeyBytes != \"\" {\n\t\tmethod, err = gitssh.NewPublicKeys(\n\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.User,\n\t\t\t[]byte(auth.SSHAuth.PrivateKeyBytes),\n\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.Password,\n\t\t)\n\t} else {\n\t\tmethod, err = gitssh.NewPublicKeysFromFile(\n\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.User,\n\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.PrivateKeyPath,\n\t\t\tauth.SSHAuth.Password,\n\t\t)\n\t}\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tif auth.SSHAuth.InsecureIgnoreHostKey {\n\t\tmethod.HostKeyCallback = ssh.InsecureIgnoreHostKey()\n\t}\n\topts = append(opts, git.WithAuth(method))\n}\n```\n\n**Key patterns**:\n- Uses mutually exclusive switch with multiple auth types\n- Converts config types to go-git transport types\n- Uses options pattern (`git.WithAuth()`) to configure Source\n- Handles both static credentials and file-based credentials (for SSH keys)\n\n### 5. Git Storage Source Implementation (internal/storage/fs/git/source.go)\n\nThe Source struct stores the auth method as a `transport.AuthMethod` interface:\n\n```go\ntype Source struct {\n\tlogger *zap.Logger\n\trepo   *git.Repository\n\turl    string\n\tref    string\n\thash   plumbing.Hash\n\tinterval time.Duration\n\tauth   transport.AuthMethod  // <-- Accepts any auth method\n}\n\n// WithAuth returns an option which configures the auth method used by the provided source\nfunc WithAuth(auth transport.AuthMethod) containers.Option[Source] {\n\treturn func(s *Source) {\n\t\ts.auth = auth\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSource uses auth in both Clone and Fetch\nfunc (s *Source) NewSource(...) {\n\tsource.repo, err = git.Clone(memory.NewStorage(), nil, &git.CloneOptions{\n\t\tAuth: source.auth,  // <-- Passed to Clone\n\t\tURL:  source.url,\n\t})\n}\n\n// Subscribe also uses auth for periodic Fetch operations\nfunc (s *Source) Subscribe(ctx context.Context, ch chan<- fs.FS) {\n\t// ... in ticker loop:\n\tif err := s.repo.Fetch(&git.FetchOptions{\n\t\tAuth: s.auth,  // <-- Reused for Fetch\n\t\t// ...\n\t})\n}\n```\n\n### 6. Let me create a comprehensive summary document.\n\nBased on my thorough search of the Flipt codebase, I've identified the key patterns and implementations for AWS and other cloud credential handling. Here's my comprehensive report:\n\n## Summary of AWS Credentials and Authentication Handling in Flipt\n\n### 1. **AWS SDK v2 Integration (internal/storage/fs/s3/)**\n\n**File: `/workspace/internal/storage/fs/s3/source.go`**\n\nThe codebase uses **AWS SDK v2** with a credential chain approach:\n\n```go\nimport (\n    \"github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/config\"\n    \"github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/s3\"\n)\n\n// Credential loading happens via config.LoadDefaultConfig()\ncfg, err := config.LoadDefaultConfig(context.Background(), s3opts...)\ns.s3 = s3.NewFromConfig(cfg)\n```\n\n**Key patterns:**\n- Uses `config.LoadDefaultConfig()` which automatically handles AWS credential chains (environment variables, IAM roles, credentials file, etc.)\n- Supports optional region and custom endpoint configuration\n- Configuration options are built using functional options pattern: `[]func(*config.LoadOptions) error`\n\n### 2. **Source Architecture Pattern**\n\nAll storage sources follow the same interface pattern defined in `/workspace/internal/storage/fs/store.go`:\n\n```go\ntype FSSource interface {\n    fmt.Stringer\n    Get() (fs.FS, error)\n    Subscribe(context.Context, chan<- fs.FS)\n}\n```\n\n**Sources implemented:**\n- **S3** (`/workspace/internal/storage/fs/s3/source.go`) - Uses AWS SDK v2\n- **Git** (`/workspace/internal/storage/fs/git/source.go`) - Uses go-git with pluggable auth\n- **OCI** (`/workspace/internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go`) - Uses ORAS library\n- **Local** (`/workspace/internal/storage/fs/local/source.go`) - No auth needed\n- **Database** - Separate SQL-based storage\n\n### 3. **Options Pattern for Configuration**\n\n**File: `/workspace/internal/containers/option.go`**\n\nGeneric options pattern used throughout:\n\n```go\ntype Option[T any] func(*T)\n\nfunc ApplyAll[T any](t *T, opts ...Option[T]) {\n    for _, opt := range opts {\n        opt(t)\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Example from S3:**\n```go\nfunc WithRegion(region string) containers.Option[Source] {\n    return func(s *Source) {\n        s.region = region\n    }\n}\n\nfunc WithEndpoint(endpoint string) containers.Option[Source] {\n    return func(s *Source) {\n        s.endpoint = endpoint\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### 4. **Git Authentication Pattern** (Highly Reusable)\n\n**File: `/workspace/internal/storage/fs/git/source.go`**\n\nThe git implementation shows how to handle multiple authentication types:\n\n```go\ntype Source struct {\n    logger *zap.Logger\n    repo   *git.Repository\n    url    string\n    ref    string\n    auth   transport.AuthMethod  // Pluggable auth interface\n}\n\n// WithAuth returns an option which configures the auth method\nfunc WithAuth(auth transport.AuthMethod) containers.Option[Source] {\n    return func(s *Source) {\n        s.auth = auth\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Integration in grpc.go (lines 154-198):**\nSupports multiple auth methods:\n- **BasicAuth** - Username/password\n- **TokenAuth** - Access tokens\n- **SSHAuth** - SSH key-based auth with:\n  - Private key bytes or file path\n  - Password protection\n  - Host key verification control\n\n### 5. **Token/Credential Refresh Patterns**\n\n**File: `/workspace/internal/storage/fs/store.go`**\n\nThe Store manages polling and subscription for updates:\n\n```go\nfunc (s *Source) Subscribe(ctx context.Context, ch chan<- fs.FS) {\n    defer close(ch)\n    \n    ticker := time.NewTicker(s.interval)\n    for {\n        select {\n        case <-ctx.Done():\n            return\n        case <-ticker.C:\n            fs, err := s.Get()\n            if err != nil {\n                s.logger.Error(\"error getting file system\", zap.Error(err))\n                continue\n            }\n            ch <- fs\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key insight:** Each source re-invokes `Get()` on every poll interval, which means:\n- AWS SDK's credential chain is re-evaluated on each refresh\n- New temporary credentials are fetched automatically\n- No explicit token refresh logic needed - relies on SDK's built-in credential caching and refresh\n\n### 6. **Configuration Structure**\n\n**File: `/workspace/internal/config/storage.go`**\n\nThe storage configuration supports:\n\n```go\ntype StorageConfig struct {\n    Type     StorageType\n    Local    *Local\n    Git      *Git\n    Object   *Object  // Contains S3\n    OCI      *OCI\n}\n\ntype S3 struct {\n    Endpoint     string        // Optional custom endpoint\n    Bucket       string        // Required\n    Prefix       string        // Optional prefix\n    Region       string        // Optional region\n    PollInterval time.Duration // Default 1 minute\n}\n\ntype Authentication struct {\n    BasicAuth *BasicAuth\n    TokenAuth *TokenAuth\n    SSHAuth   *SSHAuth\n}\n```\n\n### 7. **Server Initialization Pattern**\n\n**File: `/workspace/internal/cmd/grpc.go` (lines 469-496)**\n\nShows how sources are instantiated:\n\n```go\ncase config.S3ObjectSubStorageType:\n    opts := []containers.Option[s3.Source]{\n        s3.WithPollInterval(objectCfg.S3.PollInterval),\n    }\n    if objectCfg.S3.Endpoint != \"\" {\n        opts = append(opts, s3.WithEndpoint(objectCfg.S3.Endpoint))\n    }\n    if objectCfg.S3.Region != \"\" {\n        opts = append(opts, s3.WithRegion(objectCfg.S3.Region))\n    }\n    source, err := s3.NewSource(logger, objectCfg.S3.Bucket, opts...)\n    store, err = fs.NewStore(logger, source)\n```\n\n### 8. **OCI/ORAS Current Implementation**\n\n**File: `/workspace/internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go`**\n\nCurrently minimal - ORAS Library Authentication Overview\n\n**Current ORAS Version:** `oras.land/oras-go/v2 v2.3.1` (in `/workspace/go.mod:214`)\n\n**Authentication Mechanism:** ORAS uses the `registry/remote/auth` package with an HTTP client-based authentication model through the `auth.Client` struct.\n\n---\n\n### 2. Authentication Types Supported in ORAS\n\nThe ORAS `remote.Repository` struct supports authentication through the `Client` field, which wraps HTTP requests with authentication. The `auth.Client` supports:\n\n**Primary Authentication Methods:**\n\n1. **Basic Authentication** (Username/Password)\n   - Via `auth.StaticCredential()` with `Username` and `Password` fields\n   - Uses HTTP Basic Auth scheme (RFC 7617)\n\n2. **Bearer Token/Access Token**\n   - Via `auth.StaticCredential()` with `AccessToken` field\n   - Uses Bearer scheme (RFC 6750)\n\n3. **Refresh Token** (OAuth2)\n   - Via `auth.StaticCredential()` with `RefreshToken` field\n   - Used for fetching new access tokens\n\n4. **No Authentication** (Public registries)\n   - Plain HTTP requests without credentials\n\n**Credential Struct Fields:**\n```go\ntype Credential struct {\n    Username string      // Basic auth username\n    Password string      // Basic auth password\n    RefreshToken string  // OAuth2 refresh token\n    AccessToken string   // Bearer token\n}\n```\n\n**Additional Auth Features:**\n- **Credential Cache** - `auth.DefaultCache` to avoid repeated authentication\n- **Scope Support** - Can specify Docker registry scopes (e.g., `repository:name:pull,push`)\n- **Force OAuth2** - `ForceAttemptOAuth2` flag to enforce OAuth2 instead of distribution spec\n- **Custom User Agent** - `SetUserAgent()` method\n\n---\n\n### 3. How Flipt Currently Uses ORAS\n\n**Files Involved:**\n- `/workspace/internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go` - OCI repository source implementation\n- `/workspace/internal/oci/store.go` - OCI storage operations (Push/Pull)\n- `/workspace/internal/cmd/grpc.go` - Server initialization with OCI storage\n- `/workspace/internal/config/storage.go` - Configuration structure for OCI\n\n**Current Implementation:**\n\nIn `/workspace/internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go:38-61`:\n```go\nfunc NewSource(logger *zap.Logger, reference string, opts ...containers.Option[Source]) (_ *Source, err error) {\n    ref, err := registry.ParseReference(reference)\n    if err != nil {\n        return nil, err\n    }\n\n    repo, err := remote.NewRepository(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", ref.Registry, ref.Repository))\n    if err != nil {\n        return nil, err\n    }\n\n    repo.PlainHTTP = true  // Currently hardcoded to Plain HTTP\n    \n    // ... rest of initialization\n}\n```\n\nSimilar patterns in:\n- `/workspace/internal/oci/store.go:169` - Push operation\n- `/workspace/internal/oci/store.go:199` - Pull operation\n\n**Current Limitations:**\n1. **No Authentication Support** - All remote repositories are created without any auth client\n2. **PlainHTTP Hardcoded** - All connections use plain HTTP instead of HTTPS\n3. **No Configuration** - The `OCI` struct in config only has a `Repository` field, no auth config\n\n---\n\n### 4. SSH key handling\n}\n```\n\n**Pattern for OCI:**\n- The `oci.Source` struct needs to accept auth configuration via options\n- A `WithAuth()` option function is needed (similar to Git's)\n- The auth must be set on `repository.Client` field\n\n---\n\n### 5. ORAS Library Authentication Overview\n\n**Current ORAS Version:** `oras.land/oras-go/v2 v2.3.1` (in `/workspace/go.mod:214`)\n\n**Authentication Mechanism:** ORAS uses the `registry/remote/auth` package with an HTTP client-based authentication model through the `auth.Client` struct.\n\n---\n\n### 2. Authentication Types Supported in ORAS\n\nThe ORAS `remote.Repository` struct supports authentication through the `Client` field, which wraps HTTP requests with authentication. The `auth.Client` supports:\n\n**Primary Authentication Methods:**\n\n1. **Basic Authentication** (Username/Password)\n   - Via `auth.StaticCredential()` with `Username` and `Password` fields\n   - Uses HTTP Basic Auth scheme (RFC 7617)\n\n2. **Bearer Token/Access Token**\n   - Via `auth.StaticCredential()` with `AccessToken` field\n   - Uses Bearer scheme (RFC 6750)\n\n3. **Refresh Token** (OAuth2)\n   - Via `auth.StaticCredential()` with `RefreshToken` field\n   - Used for fetching new access tokens\n\n4. **No Authentication** (Public registries)\n   - Plain HTTP requests without credentials\n\n**Credential Struct Fields:**\n```go\ntype Credential struct {\n    Username string      // Basic auth username\n    Password string      // Basic auth password\n    RefreshToken string  // OAuth2 refresh token\n    AccessToken string   // Bearer token\n}\n```\n\n**Additional Auth Features:**\n- **Credential Cache** - `auth.DefaultCache` to avoid repeated authentication\n- **Scope Support** - Can specify Docker registry scopes (e.g., `repository:name:pull,push`)\n- **Force OAuth2** - `ForceAttemptOAuth2` flag to enforce OAuth2 instead of distribution spec\n- **Custom User Agent** - `SetUserAgent()` method\n\n---\n\n### 3. How Flipt Currently Uses ORAS\n\n**Files Involved:**\n- `/workspace/internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go` - OCI repository source implementation\n- `/workspace/internal/oci/store.go` - OCI storage operations (Push/Pull)\n- `/workspace/internal/cmd/grpc.go` - Server initialization with OCI storage\n- `/workspace/internal/config/storage.go` - Configuration structure for OCI\n\n**Current Implementation:**\n\nIn `/workspace/internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go:38-61`:\n```go\nfunc NewSource(logger *zap.Logger, reference string, opts ...containers.Option[Source]) (_ *Source, err error) {\n    ref, err := registry.ParseReference(reference)\n    if err != nil {\n        return nil, err\n    }\n\n    repo, err := remote.NewRepository(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", ref.Registry, ref.Repository))\n    if err != nil {\n        return nil, err\n    }\n\n    repo.PlainHTTP = true  // Currently hardcoded to Plain HTTP\n    \n    // ... rest of initialization\n}\n```\n\nSimilar patterns in:\n- `/workspace/internal/oci/store.go:169` - Push operation\n- `/workspace/internal/oci/store.go:199` - Pull operation\n\n**Current Limitations:**\n1. **No Authentication Support** - All remote repositories are created without any auth client\n2. **PlainHTTP Hardcoded** - All connections use plain HTTP instead of HTTPS\n3. **No Configuration** - The `OCI` struct in config only has a `Repository` field, no auth config\n\n---\n\n### 4. How Git Authentication is Handled (Pattern to Follow)\n\nIn `/workspace/internal/cmd/grpc.go:154-198`, Git authentication is properly configured:\n\n**Git Config Structure** (`/workspace/internal/config/storage.go:109-155`):\n```go\ntype Git struct {\n    Repository     string\n    Ref            string\n    PollInterval   time.Duration\n    Authentication Authentication  // <- Contains auth details\n}\n\ntype Authentication struct {\n    BasicAuth *BasicAuth  // username + password\n    TokenAuth *TokenAuth  // access token\n    SSHAuth   *SSHAuth    // SSH key + passphrase\n}\n```\n\n**Git Authentication Implementation** (`/workspace/internal/cmd/grpc.go:160-198`):\n```go\nauth := cfg.Storage.Git.Authentication\nswitch {\ncase auth.BasicAuth != nil:\n    opts = append(opts, git.WithAuth(&http.BasicAuth{\n        Username: auth.BasicAuth.Username,\n        Password: auth.BasicAuth.Password,\n    }))\ncase auth.TokenAuth != nil:\n    opts = append(opts, git.WithAuth(&http.TokenAuth{\n        Token: auth.TokenAuth.AccessToken,\n    }))\ncase auth.SSHAuth != nil:\n    // ... SSH key handling\n}\n```\n\n**Pattern for OCI:**\n- The `oci.Source` struct needs to accept auth configuration via options\n- A `WithAuth()` option function is needed (similar to Git's)\n- The auth must be set on `repository.Client` field\n\n---\n\n### 5. What's Needed for OCI Authentication in Flipt\n\n**1. Configuration Changes** (`internal/config/storage.go`):\n   - Extend `OCI` struct to include authentication fields (BasicAuth, TokenAuth, similar to Git)\n   - OR extend `Authentication` struct to support OCI-specific needs\n\n**2. Source Implementation** (`internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go`):\n   - Add authentication field to `Source` struct\n   - Create `WithAuth()` option function to accept auth configuration\n   - Create `WithBasicAuth()`, `WithTokenAuth()` convenience functions (matching Git pattern)\n   - Set the auth client on `repo.Client` field using `auth.Client` from ORAS\n\n**3. Server Initialization** (`internal/cmd/grpc.go:224-233`):\n   - Add auth handling logic similar to Git storage (lines 160-198)\n   - Parse OCI auth from config\n   - Pass auth options to `oci.NewSource()`\n\n**4. Store Implementation** (`internal/oci/store.go`):\n   - Update `Push()` and `Pull()` methods to accept and use auth\n   - Consider creating a helper to apply auth to repositories\n\n---\n\n### 6. Key Code Locations and File Paths\n\n**Absolute paths:**\n- `/workspace/internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go` - OCI source (needs auth support)\n- `/workspace/internal/oci/store.go` - OCI store Push/Pull (needs auth)\n- `/workspace/internal/cmd/grpc.go` - Server init (needs auth setup) \n- `/workspace/internal/config/storage.go` - Config structures (needs auth fields)\n- `/workspace/go.mod` - Dependencies (line 214: ORAS, lines 161-162: OCI image spec)\n\n**Related Auth Examples:**\n- `/workspace/internal/storage/fs/git/source.go:38-93` - Git auth pattern\n- `/workspace/internal/cmd/grpc.go:154-208` - Git auth initialization pattern\n\n---\n\n### 7. ORAS Dependencies Already in Use\n\n```\noras.land/oras-go/v2 v2.3.1\ngithub.com/opencontainers/go-digest v1.0.0\ngithub.com/opencontainers/image-spec v1.1.0-rc5\n```\n\n**Needed imports for auth** (not yet in use):\n```go\n\"oras.land/oras-go/v2/registry/remote/auth\"\n\"oras.land/oras-go/v2/registry/remote/retry\"\n```\n\n---\n\n### Summary\n\nFlipt currently has **zero authentication support** for OCI registries. The infrastructure to support it is partially in place (ORAS library, basic config structure), but:\n\n1. **No auth configuration** - Config only stores repository URL, not credentials\n2. **No auth setup** - Server init doesn't handle OCI auth setup\n3. **No auth options** - OCI Source doesn't have WithAuth() or similar options\n4. **Hardcoded PlainHTTP** - All connections bypass HTTPS security\n\nThe Git storage implementation (`internal/storage/fs/git/`) provides an excellent pattern to follow for implementing OCI authentication support."}],"totalDurationMs":87047,"totalTokens":56249,"totalToolUseCount":53,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":792,"cache_read_input_tokens":53390,"output_tokens":2067,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":792,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

Only static `username/password` authentication is supported today; AWS-issued tokens expire (~12h), and after expiry, pulls fail until credentials are manually rotated.\n\n**Solution Approach**: Add support for AWS credentials chain authentication in OCI storage, with automatic token refresh through periodic re-pulling (leveraging existing polling infrastructure).\n\n**Scope**:\n- Add authentication configuration options to OCI storage (static + AWS provider chain)\n- Implement AWS ECR authentication using AWS SDK v2\n- Leverage existing polling mechanism for automatic token refresh\n- Maintain backward compatibility with static credentials\n- Follow established Flipt patterns (Git storage auth as a model)\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n### Phase 1: Configuration Changes\n**File**: `internal/config/storage.go`\n\n1. **Extend OCI struct** to include authentication options (following Git storage pattern):\n   - Add `Authentication` field to OCI struct\n   - Define `OCIAuthentication` struct with:\n     - `BasicAuth` (*BasicAuth) - for static username/password or ECR static credentials\n     - `TokenAuth` (*TokenAuth) - for bearer tokens\n     - `AWSAuth` (*AWSAuth) - NEW, for AWS credential chain (ECR)\n\n2. **Define AWSAuth struct** with:\n   ```go\n   type AWSAuth struct {\n       Region       string  // Optional, auto-detected if not provided\n       UseChain     bool    // If true, use AWS credential chain (env vars, IAM role, etc.)\n   }\n   ```\n\n3. **Add validation** for auth exclusivity (only one auth type at a time)\n\n4. **Add setDefaults** for OCI polling interval (similar to S3)\n\n### Phase 2: OCI Source Authentication Support\n**File**: `internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go`\n\n1. **Extend Source struct** to store auth:\n   - Add `auth *oras.auth.Client` field (or use ORAS's auth mechanism)\n   - Keep reference to auth credentials for refresh on poll\n\n2. **Implement WithAuth() option function**:\n   ```go\n   func WithAuth(auth AuthProvider) containers.Option[Source]\n   func WithAWSAuth(region string) containers.Option[Source]\n   func WithBasicAuth(username, password string) containers.Option[Source]\n   ```\n\n3. **Create AuthProvider interface** to abstract different auth implementations:\n   ```go\n   type AuthProvider interface {\n       GetCredential(ctx context.Context, hostname string) (auth.Credential, error)\n   }\n   ```\n\n4. **Implement AWS auth provider**:\n   - Use AWS SDK v2 `config.LoadDefaultConfig()` for credential chain\n   - Implement GetECRAuthorizationToken() for pulling ECR authorization tokens\n   - Handle token caching with configurable TTL\n\n5. **Update NewSource()** to:\n   - Accept auth options\n   - Build ORAS `auth.Client` from AuthProvider\n   - Set client on remote.Repository\n\n6. **Update Subscribe()** (polling mechanism) to:\n   - Refresh credentials on each poll interval (automatic token refresh)\n   - This leverages existing polling - no new infrastructure needed\n\n### Phase 3: Server Initialization\n**File**: `internal/cmd/grpc.go`\n\n1. **Add OCI auth handling** in the storage initialization section (around lines 224-233):\n   - Parse OCI authentication from config\n   - Create AuthProvider based on auth type:\n     - BasicAuth → static credential provider\n     - AWSAuth → AWS credential chain provider\n   - Pass auth options to `oci.NewSource()`\n\n2. **Follow Git auth pattern** (lines 160-198) but adapted for OCI:\n   ```go\n   if cfg.Storage.OCI.Authentication.BasicAuth != nil {\n       opts = append(opts, oci.WithBasicAuth(...))\n   }\n   if cfg.Storage.OCI.Authentication.AWSAuth != nil {\n       opts = append(opts, oci.WithAWSAuth(...))\n   }\n   ```\n\n### Phase 4: OCI Store Updates\n**Files**: `internal/oci/store.go`, `internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go`\n\n1. **Push/Pull operations** should use authenticated repositories:\n   - Update `Push()` method to use auth from Source\n   - Update `Pull()` method to use auth from Source\n   - Pass remote.Repository with auth client to oras.Copy()\n\n2. **Token refresh mechanism**:\n   - Leverage Subscribe() polling - each poll calls Get() which re-evaluates credentials\n   - AWS SDK handles credential caching and refresh automatically\n   - No explicit token refresh code needed\n\n### Phase 5: Testing & Validation\n**Files**: Create/update test files\n\n1. **Unit tests** for:\n   - OCI config validation (mutually exclusive auth types)\n   - AWS auth provider credential fetching (mocked)\n   - Static auth provider creation\n\n2. **Integration tests** for:\n   - Push/Pull with static credentials\n   - Push/Pull with AWS auth (mocked AWS SDK)\n   - Token refresh behavior (verify re-auth on poll)\n\n---\n\n## Critical Implementation Files\n\n| File | Changes | Purpose |\n|------|---------|---------|\n| `internal/config/storage.go` | Add OCI authentication fields, AWSAuth struct, validation | Configuration structure |\n| `internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go` | Add auth support, options, AuthProvider interface, AWS integration | OCI source with auth |\n| `internal/cmd/grpc.go` | Add OCI auth initialization (lines ~224-233) | Dependency injection/setup |\n| `internal/oci/store.go` | Update Push/Pull to use auth from source | OCI operations |\n| `internal/oci/fs/fs.go` | May need minor updates for auth-aware operations | OCI filesystem |\n\n---\n\n## Reusable Patterns & Functions\n\n**From Git Storage (internal/storage/fs/git/)**:\n- Authentication struct pattern (BasicAuth, TokenAuth, Optional auth)\n- Validation pattern for auth type exclusivity\n- WithAuth() options pattern\n- Config-to-transport conversion pattern\n\n**From AWS S3 (internal/storage/fs/s3/)**:\n- AWS SDK v2 credential chain pattern: `config.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx, opts...)`\n- Options pattern for cloud-specific configuration\n\n**Existing Infrastructure**:\n- `containers.Option[T]` pattern (internal/containers/option.go)\n- Polling/Subscribe mechanism (internal/storage/fs/store.go)\n- ORAS library already in dependencies (oras.land/oras-go/v2 v2.3.1)\n\n---\n\n## Backward Compatibility\n\n- **Static credentials unchanged**: Existing OCI configs with basic auth continue to work\n- **No breaking changes**: New AWSAuth is optional, defaulting to no-auth behavior\n- **Gradual migration**: Users can switch from basic auth to AWS auth without downtime\n\n---\n\n## Verification\n\n1. **Existing tests pass**: Run `go test ./internal/config/...` and `go test ./internal/storage/fs/oci/...`\n2. **New tests for auth**: Unit tests for config validation, auth provider creation\n3. **Integration test**: Create a test that:\n   - Mocks AWS credentials provider\n support\n- `oras.land/oras-go/v2` - ORAS library with auth support\n- `github.com/opencontainers/image-spec` - OCI image spec\n\n---\n\n## Success Criteria\n\n1. ✅ OCI storage supports static {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01Pwe8pNyEcVpomumWWztvUQ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_012Gxwgm94M4d5tiVUUonjZG","name":"ExitPlanMode","input":{"plan":"# Implementation Plan: Dynamic AWS ECR Authentication for OCI Bundles\n\n## Context\n\n**Problem**: Flipt configured with OCI storage cannot continuously pull bundles from AWS ECR when using temporary credentials. Only static `username/password` authentication is supported today; AWS-issued tokens expire (~12h), and after expiry, pulls fail until credentials are manually rotated.\n\n**Solution Approach**: Add support for AWS credentials chain authentication in OCI storage, with automatic token refresh through periodic re-pulling (leveraging existing polling infrastructure).\n\n**Scope**:\n- Add authentication configuration options to OCI storage (static + AWS provider chain)\n- Implement AWS ECR authentication using AWS SDK v2\n- Leverage existing polling mechanism for automatic token refresh\n- Maintain backward compatibility with static credentials\n- Follow established Flipt patterns (Git storage auth as a model)\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n### Phase 1: Configuration Changes\n**File**: `internal/config/storage.go`\n\n1. **Extend OCI struct** to include authentication options (following Git storage pattern):\n   - Add `Authentication` field to OCI struct\n   - Define `OCIAuthentication` struct with:\n     - `BasicAuth` (*BasicAuth) - for static username/password or ECR static credentials\n     - `TokenAuth` (*TokenAuth) - for bearer tokens\n     - `AWSAuth` (*AWSAuth) - NEW, for AWS credential chain (ECR)\n\n2. **Define AWSAuth struct** with:\n   ```go\n   type AWSAuth struct {\n       Region       string  // Optional, auto-detected if not provided\n       UseChain     bool    // If true, use AWS credential chain (env vars, IAM role, etc.)\n   }\n   ```\n\n3. **Add validation** for auth exclusivity (only one auth type at a time)\n\n4. **Add setDefaults** for OCI polling interval (similar to S3)\n\n### Phase 2: OCI Source Authentication Support\n**File**: `internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go`\n\n1. **Extend Source struct** to store auth:\n   - Add `auth *oras.auth.Client` field (or use ORAS's auth mechanism)\n   - Keep reference to auth credentials for refresh on poll\n\n2. **Implement WithAuth() option function**:\n   ```go\n   func WithAuth(auth AuthProvider) containers.Option[Source]\n   func WithAWSAuth(region string) containers.Option[Source]\n   func WithBasicAuth(username, password string) containers.Option[Source]\n   ```\n\n3. **Create AuthProvider interface** to abstract different auth implementations:\n   ```go\n   type AuthProvider interface {\n       GetCredential(ctx context.Context, hostname string) (auth.Credential, error)\n   }\n   ```\n\n4. **Implement AWS auth provider**:\n   - Use AWS SDK v2 `config.LoadDefaultConfig()` for credential chain\n   - Implement GetECRAuthorizationToken() for pulling ECR authorization tokens\n   - Handle token caching with configurable TTL\n\n5. **Update NewSource()** to:\n   - Accept auth options\n   - Build ORAS `auth.Client` from AuthProvider\n   - Set client on remote.Repository\n\n6. **Update Subscribe()** (polling mechanism) to:\n   - Refresh credentials on each poll interval (automatic token refresh)\n   - This leverages existing polling - no new infrastructure needed\n\n### Phase 3: Server Initialization\n**File**: `internal/cmd/grpc.go`\n\n1. **Add OCI auth handling** in the storage initialization section (around lines 224-233):\n   - Parse OCI authentication from config\n   - Create AuthProvider based on auth type:\n     - BasicAuth → static credential provider\n     - AWSAuth → AWS credential chain provider\n   - Pass auth options to `oci.NewSource()`\n\n2. **Follow Git auth pattern** (lines 160-198) but adapted for OCI:\n   ```go\n   if cfg.Storage.OCI.Authentication.BasicAuth != nil {\n       opts = append(opts, oci.WithBasicAuth(...))\n   }\n   if cfg.Storage.OCI.Authentication.AWSAuth != nil {\n       opts = append(opts, oci.WithAWSAuth(...))\n   }\n   ```\n\n### Phase 4: OCI Store Updates\n**Files**: `internal/oci/store.go`, `internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go`\n\n1. **Push/Pull operations** should use authenticated repositories:\n   - Update `Push()` method to use auth from Source\n   - Update `Pull()` method to use auth from Source\n   - Pass remote.Repository with auth client to oras.Copy()\n\n2. **Token refresh mechanism**:\n   - Leverage Subscribe() polling - each poll calls Get() which re-evaluates credentials\n   - AWS SDK handles credential caching and refresh automatically\n   - No explicit token refresh code needed\n\n### Phase 5: Testing & Validation\n**Files**: Create/update test files\n\n1. **Unit tests** for:\n   - OCI config validation (mutually exclusive auth types)\n   - AWS auth provider credential fetching (mocked)\n   - Static auth provider creation\n\n2. **Integration tests** for:\n   - Push/Pull with static credentials\n   - Push/Pull with AWS auth (mocked AWS SDK)\n   - Token refresh behavior (verify re-auth on poll)\n\n---\n\n## Critical Implementation Files\n\n| File | Changes | Purpose |\n|------|---------|---------|\n| `internal/config/storage.go` | Add OCI authentication fields, AWSAuth struct, validation | Configuration structure |\n| `internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go` | Add auth support, options, AuthProvider interface, AWS integration | OCI source with auth |\n| `internal/cmd/grpc.go` | Add OCI auth initialization (lines ~224-233) | Dependency injection/setup |\n| `internal/oci/store.go` | Update Push/Pull to use auth from source | OCI operations |\n| `internal/oci/fs/fs.go` | May need minor updates for auth-aware operations | OCI filesystem |\n\n---\n\n## Reusable Patterns & Functions\n\n**From Git Storage (internal/storage/fs/git/)**:\n- Authentication struct pattern (BasicAuth, TokenAuth, Optional auth)\n- Validation pattern for auth type exclusivity\n- WithAuth() options pattern\n- Config-to-transport conversion pattern\n\n**From AWS S3 (internal/storage/fs/s3/)**:\n- AWS SDK v2 credential chain pattern: `config.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx, opts...)`\n- Options pattern for cloud-specific configuration\n\n**Existing Infrastructure**:\n- `containers.Option[T]` pattern (internal/containers/option.go)\n- Polling/Subscribe mechanism (internal/storage/fs/store.go)\n- ORAS library already in dependencies (oras.land/oras-go/v2 v2.3.1)\n\n---\n\n## Backward Compatibility\n\n- **Static credentials unchanged**: Existing OCI configs with basic auth continue to work\n- **No breaking changes**: New AWSAuth is optional, defaulting to no-auth behavior\n- **Gradual migration**: Users can switch from basic auth to AWS auth without downtime\n\n---\n\n## Verification\n\n1. **Existing tests pass**: Run `go test ./internal/config/...` and `go test ./internal/storage/fs/oci/...`\n2. **New tests for auth**: Unit tests for config validation, auth provider creation\n3. **Integration test**: Create a test that:\n   - Mocks AWS credentials provider\n   - Creates OCI source with AWS auth\n   - Verifies credentials are fetched\n   - Verifies Pull/Push operations work\n4. **Manual testing**: Deploy and test against real ECR registry (optional, for final validation)\n\n---\n\n## Key Dependencies Already Available\n\n- `github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2` - AWS SDK v2 (already in go.mod)\n- `github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/config` - Credential chain support\n- `oras.land/oras-go/v2` - ORAS library with auth support\n- `github.com/opencontainers/image-spec` - OCI image spec\n\n---\n\n## Success Criteria\n\n1. ✅ OCI storage supports static (username/password) authentication\n2. ✅ OCI storage supports AWS credential chain authentication\n3. ✅ Tokens refresh automatically on each poll interval\n4. ✅ Configuration accepts new auth type selection\n5. ✅ Backward compatibility maintained (static auth still works)\n6. ✅ All existing tests pass\n7. ✅ New tests cover auth functionality\n\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5392,"cache_read_input_tokens":102109,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":5392},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9242c96e-345e-487b-a52c-90157cb0ee2d","uuid":"9f9992f3-1ee3-40d8-ac3c-a08691293c38"}

support for AWS credentials chain authentication in OCI storage, with automatic token refresh through periodic re-pulling (leveraging existing polling infrastructure).\n\n**Scope**:\n- Add authentication configuration options to OCI storage (static + AWS provider chain)\n- Implement AWS ECR authentication using AWS SDK v2\n- Leverage existing polling mechanism for automatic token refresh\n- Maintain backward compatibility with static credentials\n- Follow established Flipt patterns (Git storage auth as a model)\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n### Phase 1: Configuration Changes\n**File**: `internal/config/storage.go`\n\n1. **Extend OCI struct** to include authentication options (following Git storage pattern):\n   - Add `Authentication` field to OCI struct\n   - Define `OCIAuthentication` struct with:\n     - `BasicAuth` (*BasicAuth) - for static username/password or ECR static credentials\n     - `TokenAuth` (*TokenAuth) - for bearer tokens\n     - `AWSAuth` (*AWSAuth) - NEW, for AWS credential chain (ECR)\n\n2. **Define AWSAuth struct** with:\n   ```go\n   type AWSAuth struct {\n       Region       string  // Optional, auto-detected if not provided\n       UseChain     bool    // If true, use AWS credential chain (env vars, IAM role, etc.)\n   }\n   ```\n\n3. **Add validation** for auth exclusivity (only one auth type at a time)\n\n4. **Add setDefaults** for OCI polling interval (similar to S3)\n\n### Phase 2: OCI Source Authentication Support\n**File**: `internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go`\n\n1. **Extend Source struct** to store auth:\n   - Add `auth *oras.auth.Client` field (or use ORAS's auth mechanism)\n   - Keep reference to auth credentials for refresh on poll\n\n2. **Implement WithAuth() option function**:\n   ```go\n   func WithAuth(auth AuthProvider) containers.Option[Source]\n   func WithAWSAuth(region string) containers.Option[Source]\n   func WithBasicAuth(username, password string) containers.Option[Source]\n   ```\n\n3. **Create AuthProvider interface** to abstract different auth implementations:\n   ```go\n   type AuthProvider interface {\n       GetCredential(ctx context.Context, hostname string) (auth.Credential, error)\n   }\n   ```\n\n4. **Implement AWS auth provider**:\n   - Use AWS SDK v2 `config.LoadDefaultConfig()` for credential chain\n   - Implement GetECRAuthorizationToken() for pulling ECR authorization tokens\n   - Handle token caching with configurable TTL\n\n5. **Update NewSource()** to:\n   - Accept auth options\n   - Build ORAS `auth.Client` from AuthProvider\n   - Set client on remote.Repository\n\n6. **Update Subscribe()** (polling mechanism) to:\n   - Refresh credentials on each poll interval (automatic token refresh)\n   - This leverages existing polling - no new infrastructure needed\n\n### Phase 3: Server Initialization\n**File**: `internal/cmd/grpc.go`\n\n1. **Add OCI auth handling** in the storage initialization section (around lines 224-233):\n   - Parse OCI authentication from config\n   - Create AuthProvider based on auth type:\n     - BasicAuth → static credential provider\n     - AWSAuth → AWS credential chain provider\n   - Pass auth options to `oci.NewSource()`\n\n2. **Follow Git auth pattern** (lines 160-198) but adapted for OCI:\n   ```go\n   if cfg.Storage.OCI.Authentication.BasicAuth != nil {\n       opts = append(opts, oci.WithBasicAuth(...))\n   }\n   if cfg.Storage.OCI.Authentication.AWSAuth != nil {\n       opts = append(opts, oci.WithAWSAuth(...))\n   }\n   ```\n\n### Phase 4: OCI Store Updates\n**Files**: `internal/oci/store.go`, `internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go`\n\n1. **Push/Pull operations** should use authenticated repositories:\n   - Update `Push()` method to use auth from Source\n   - Update `Pull()` method to use auth from Source\n   - Pass remote.Repository with auth client to oras.Copy()\n\n2. **Token refresh mechanism**:\n   - Leverage Subscribe() polling - each poll calls Get() which re-evaluates credentials\n   - AWS SDK handles credential caching and refresh automatically\n   - No explicit token refresh code needed\n\n### Phase 5: Testing & Validation\n**Files**: Create/update test files\n\n1. **Unit tests** for:\n   - OCI config validation (mutually exclusive auth types)\n   - AWS auth provider credential fetching (mocked)\n   - Static auth provider creation\n\n2. **Integration tests** for:\n   - Push/Pull with static credentials\n   - Push/Pull with AWS auth (mocked AWS SDK)\n   - Token refresh behavior (verify re-auth on poll)\n\n---\n\n## Critical Implementation Files\n\n| File | Changes | Purpose |\n|------|---------|---------|\n| `internal/config/storage.go` | Add OCI authentication fields, AWSAuth struct, validation | Configuration structure |\n| `internal/storage/fs/oci/source.go` | Add auth support, options, AuthProvider interface, AWS integration | OCI source with auth |\n| `internal/cmd/grpc.go` | Add OCI auth initialization (lines ~224-233) | Dependency injection/setup |\n| `internal/oci/store.go` | Update Push/Pull to use auth from source | OCI operations |\n| `internal/oci/fs/fs.go` | May need minor updates for auth-aware operations | OCI filesystem |\n\n---\n\n## Reusable Patterns & Functions\n\n**From Git Storage (internal/storage/fs/git/)**:\n- Authentication struct pattern (BasicAuth, TokenAuth, Optional auth)\n- Validation pattern for auth type exclusivity\n- WithAuth() options pattern\n- Config-to-transport conversion pattern\n\n**From AWS S3 (internal/storage/fs/s3/)**:\n- AWS SDK v2 credential chain pattern: `config.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx, opts...)`\n- Options pattern for cloud-specific configuration\n\n**Existing Infrastructure**:\n- `containers.Option[T]` pattern (internal/containers/option.go)\n- Polling/Subscribe mechanism (internal/storage/fs/store.go)\n- ORAS library already in dependencies (oras.land/oras-go/v2 v2.3.1)\n\n---\n\n## Backward Compatibility\n\n- **Static credentials unchanged**: Existing OCI configs with basic auth continue to work\n- **No breaking changes**: New AWSAuth is optional, defaulting to no-auth behavior\n- **Gradual migration**: Users can switch from basic auth to AWS auth without downtime\n\n---\n\n## Verification\n\n1. **Existing tests pass**: Run `go test ./internal/config/...` and `go test ./internal/storage/fs/oci/...`\n2. **New tests for auth**: Unit tests for config validation, auth provider creation\n3. **Integration test**: Create a test that:\n   - Mocks AWS credentials provider\n   - Creates OCI source with AWS auth\n   - Verifies credentials are fetched\n   - Verifies Pull/Push operations work\n4. **Manual testing**: Deploy and test against real ECR registry (optional, for final validation)\n\n---\n\n## Key Dependencies Already Available\n\n- `github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2` - AWS SDK v2 (already in go.mod)\n- `github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/config` - Credential chain support\n- `oras.land/oras-go/v2` - ORAS library with auth support\n- `github.com/opencontainers/image-spec` - OCI image spec\n\n---\n\n## Success Criteria\n\n1. ✅ OCI storage supports static (username/password) authentication\n2. ✅ OCI storage supports AWS credential chain authentication\n3. ✅ Tokens refresh automatically on each poll interval\n4. ✅ Configuration accepts new auth type
